indicators.desc_en: Measures the change in prices paid by urban consumers for a basket of goods and services. The year-over-year change is the most-watched inflation metric in the US. Above 2% signals elevated inflation; the Fed targets 2% over time.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量城市消費者購買商品和服務的價格變化。年增率是美國最受關注的通膨指標。超過 2% 代表通膨偏高，聯準會的長期通膨目標為 2%。

indicators.name_zh: 核心 CPI（不含食品與能源）
indicators.desc_en: CPI excluding volatile food and energy prices. Core CPI is the Fed's preferred short-term inflation gauge because it filters out supply-shock noise. Persistently high core CPI is a strong signal for rate hikes.
indicators.desc_zh: 剔除波動較大的食品和能源的 CPI。核心 CPI 是聯準會偏好的短期通膨指標，因為它過濾了供給衝擊的噪音。核心 CPI 持續偏高是升息的強烈訊號。

indicators.name_zh: 核心 PCE 物價指數
indicators.desc_en: The Fed's officially preferred inflation measure. PCE covers a broader range of spending than CPI and adjusts for substitution effects. The FOMC explicitly targets 2% Core PCE as its long-run goal.
indicators.desc_zh: 聯準會官方偏好的通膨衡量指標。PCE 涵蓋比 CPI 更廣泛的消費支出，且會調整替代效應。聯邦公開市場委員會明確以 2% 核心 PCE 為長期目標。

indicators.desc_en: Measures average change in prices received by domestic producers. PPI is a leading indicator for CPI—rising producer costs often get passed on to consumers. A surge in PPI often precedes consumer inflation by 1-3 months.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量國內生產者收到的價格平均變化。PPI 是 CPI 的領先指標，生產成本上升往往會轉嫁給消費者，PPI 飆升通常比消費通膨早 1-3 個月出現。

indicators.name_zh: 密大消費者通膨預期（一年期）
indicators.desc_en: University of Michigan survey measuring consumer expectations for inflation over the next year. Anchored inflation expectations are critical for central bank credibility. A jump above 4% raises concern about a wage-price spiral.
indicators.desc_zh: 密西根大學調查消費者對未來一年通膨的預期。通膨預期是否穩定對央行公信力至關重要，若飆破 4% 將引發工資-物價螺旋上升的疑慮。

indicators.desc_en: Number of jobs added to the economy (ex-farm & household). The most market-moving monthly release. Above +150K is generally healthy; below +100K signals slowdown. The Fed watches NFP closely alongside inflation.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國經濟新增的非農就業人數，是每月最撼動市場的數據。超過 +15 萬通常為健康；低於 +10 萬則暗示放緩，聯準會高度關注此數據。

indicators.desc_en: Percentage of the labor force that is unemployed and actively seeking work. At 3.5-4%, the labor market is considered near-full employment. The Fed's dual mandate includes maximizing employment.
indicators.desc_zh: 正在積極求職的勞動力佔比。3.5-4% 附近被認為接近充分就業。聯準會的雙重任務包含最大化就業。

indicators.name_zh: 初次申請失業救濟金人數
indicators.desc_en: Weekly count of newly unemployed individuals filing for benefits. The most timely labor market indicator. A 4-week moving average above 300K often signals labor market deterioration. Markets are sensitive to sharp week-to-week moves.
indicators.desc_zh: 每週新申請失業救濟金的人數，是最即時的勞動市場指標。四週移動平均超過 30 萬通常代表勞動市場惡化，市場對每週大幅變動極為敏感。

indicators.desc_en: Percentage of working-age population that is employed or actively job-seeking. Low participation can mask real unemployment. Post-COVID, the US LFPR has recovered but remains below pre-pandemic highs due to aging demographics.
indicators.desc_zh: 工作年齡人口中就業或積極求職的比例。低參與率可能掩蓋真實失業情況。疫情後美國勞動參與率已回升，但因人口老化，仍低於疫前高點。

indicators.desc_en: Broadest measure of labor underutilization, including part-time workers who want full-time jobs and marginally attached workers. U-6 typically runs 3-5 percentage points above U-3 (headline unemployment). A narrowing U3/U6 gap indicates a tightening labor market.
indicators.desc_zh: 最廣義的勞動力未充分利用指標，包含希望全職工作卻只能兼職者及邊緣勞工。U-6 通常比 U-3（標準失業率）高 3-5 個百分點，兩者差距收窄代表勞動市場緊俏。

indicators.desc_en: Inflation-adjusted total output of the US economy. Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth constitutes a technical recession. Healthy growth is generally 2-3% annualized. This is the definitive measure of economic health.
indicators.desc_zh: 經通膨調整的美國經濟總產出。連續兩季負成長即為技術性衰退。健康的成長率約為年化 2-3%，是最終極的經濟健康指標。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly measure of consumer spending at retail stores, excluding volatile auto sales. Consumer spending drives ~70% of US GDP, so strong retail sales signal economic resilience. A sharp miss can trigger equity market selloffs.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月零售商場消費支出，剔除波動較大的汽車銷售。消費支出佔美國 GDP 約 70%，強勁的零售銷售代表經濟韌性，大幅低於預期可能引發股市賣壓。

indicators.desc_en: Measures output of US factories, mines, and utilities. Industrial production accounts for about 15% of GDP but is more volatile and leads economic turning points. A sustained decline often precedes recession.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量美國工廠、礦業和公用事業的產出。工業生產佔 GDP 約 15%，波動較大但能領先經濟轉折點，持續下滑往往是衰退先兆。

indicators.desc_en: Total spending by US households on goods and services. PCE is broader than retail sales and is the primary component of GDP. A deceleration in PCE growth warns of economic softening even when headline unemployment stays low.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國家庭在商品和服務上的總支出，比零售銷售更廣泛，是 GDP 的主要組成部分。PCE 成長放緩是經濟趨軟的警示，即使失業率仍低。

indicators.desc_en: The interest rate at which banks lend reserve balances to each other overnight—the primary policy tool of the Federal Reserve. Rate hikes cool inflation but risk slowing growth; cuts stimulate the economy. Every basis point move ripples across all asset classes.
indicators.desc_zh: 銀行間隔夜拆借準備金的利率，是聯準會的主要政策工具。升息可抑制通膨但有壓縮成長的風險；降息可刺激經濟。每一個基點的變動都會波及所有資產類別。

indicators.desc_en: Number of new residential construction projects begun per month. Housing is a leading indicator—builders break ground based on demand expectations 6-18 months out. A drop in starts signals a cooling market well before prices adjust.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月開始動工的新住宅建設數量。房屋建設是領先指標，建商依據 6-18 個月後的需求預期動工。開工數下降是房市降溫的早期訊號，早於房價反應。

indicators.desc_en: Number of new construction permits issued monthly. Permits are issued before construction starts, making them an even more leading indicator than housing starts—often 1-2 months ahead. A permit trend closely tracks future housing supply.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月核發的新建築許可數量。許可在施工前核發，比新屋開工數更具領先性，通常早 1-2 個月反映趨勢，許可趨勢緊密追蹤未來住宅供給。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly count of completed sales of previously constructed homes (seasonally adjusted annual rate). Existing home sales account for ~85% of total home sales and reflect real-time demand. A decline signals affordability stress, usually from high mortgage rates or prices.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月已建成住宅的成交量（季調年化）。佔全美房屋銷售約 85%，反映即時需求。銷量下滑代表購房負擔壓力增大，通常源於高房貸利率或高房價。

indicators.desc_en: Average interest rate for a 30-year fixed mortgage in the US. Mortgage rates typically move with the 10-year Treasury yield plus a spread. Above 7% significantly pressures housing affordability and can freeze transaction volume.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國 30 年期固定房貸平均利率。房貸利率通常隨 10 年期美債殖利率加上利差波動，超過 7% 會顯著壓縮購房可負擔性並可能凍結成交量。

indicators.desc_en: The gold standard for tracking US home price changes across 20 major metropolitan areas using a repeat-sales methodology. Published with a 2-month lag. YoY changes above 10% signal speculative excess; consecutive monthly declines signal a correction.
indicators.desc_zh: 追蹤 20 個主要大都會區房價變化的黃金標準指標，採用重複銷售方法論。發布有約 2 個月滯後。年增率超過 10% 代表投機過熱，連續月跌代表房市修正。

indicators.desc_en: Total US manufacturing sector employment (BLS payrolls). A leading indicator for the goods-producing economy. Sustained YoY declines have historically preceded broader recessions.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國製造業總就業人數（勞工部薪資調查）。商品生產經濟的領先指標，年增率持續為負通常領先整體衰退。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly value of new orders placed for manufactured goods expected to last 3+ years (aircraft, machinery, vehicles). Highly volatile due to aircraft orders. The ex-defense, ex-aircraft 'core capital goods' measure is preferred for clean business investment trends.
indicators.desc_zh: 每月製造業新接的耐久財訂單（使用 3 年以上如飛機、機械、車輛）。因飛機訂單而波動極大。剔除國防和飛機的「核心資本財」指標更能反映乾淨的商業投資趨勢。

indicators.desc_en: Index measuring the real output of the manufacturing sector (subset of total industrial production). Captures factory floor activity in real time. A sustained decline in manufacturing production often precedes broader economic slowdowns by 2-3 quarters.
indicators.desc_zh: 衡量製造業實際產出的指數（工業生產總指數的子集）。即時捕捉工廠生產動態。製造業生產持續下滑，往往比整體經濟放緩早 2-3 季出現。

indicators.desc_en: Monthly University of Michigan survey of consumer confidence about current and future economic conditions. Acts as both a coincident and leading indicator—a sharp drop in sentiment can become self-fulfilling as consumers pull back on spending.
indicators.desc_zh: 密西根大學每月調查消費者對當前和未來經濟狀況的信心，兼具同步和領先指標功能。信心大幅下滑可能自我實現，因為消費者會縮減支出。

indicators.desc_en: The spread between 10-year and 2-year Treasury yields. An inverted curve (negative spread) has preceded every US recession since 1955 by 6-18 months. Re-steepening from inversion is often the most reliable recession imminence signal.
indicators.desc_zh: 10年期與2年期美債殖利率差。曲線倒掛（負值）自1955年以來每次都領先美國經濟衰退6-18個月。從倒掛回正陡峭化，是衰退迫近的最可靠訊號之一。

indicators.desc_en: The 10-year minus 3-month Treasury spread—the NY Fed's preferred recession indicator. Inversion has accurately predicted 8 of the last 8 US recessions. Watched more closely by economists than 10Y-2Y.
indicators.desc_zh: 10年期減3個月期美債利差，為紐約聯儲偏好的衰退指標。倒掛準確預測過去8次美國衰退中的全部8次，經濟學家比10年-2年利差更看重此指標。

indicators.desc_en: Yield on the benchmark 10-year US Treasury note. Drives mortgage rates, corporate borrowing costs, and is the global risk-free benchmark. Rising 10Y yields pressure equity valuations, especially high-duration tech stocks.
indicators.desc_zh: 10年期美國國債的基準殖利率，是房貸、企業借貸成本的指標，也是全球無風險利率基準。10年殖利率上升會壓縮股市估值，尤其衝擊長存續期的科技股。

indicators.desc_en: Yield on 2-year Treasury notes, closely tracking Fed policy expectations. The 2-year reflects what markets expect the Fed Funds rate to average over the next two years. Sharp moves often telegraph FOMC pivots before they happen.
indicators.desc_zh: 2年期美債殖利率，緊密追蹤聯準會政策預期。反映市場對未來兩年聯邦基金利率平均水準的預期，大幅波動常領先 FOMC 政策轉向。

indicators.desc_en: CBOE Volatility Index, measuring implied volatility on S&P 500 options. Below 15 = complacency; 20-30 = caution; above 40 = panic. VIX spikes correlate with sharp equity selloffs and flight-to-safety flows.
indicators.desc_zh: 芝加哥選擇權交易所波動率指數，衡量標普500選擇權的隱含波動率。低於15代表市場自滿，20-30為警戒，高於40為恐慌。VIX飆升常伴隨股市大跌與避險資金流入。

indicators.desc_en: ICE BofA US High Yield Index Option-Adjusted Spread—the premium investors demand to hold junk bonds vs. Treasuries. Below 400bps = risk-on; above 600bps = stress; above 1000bps = recession-grade panic. Leading indicator of credit cycle turns.
indicators.desc_zh: ICE BofA 美國高收益債券指數選擇權調整利差，投資人持有垃圾債相對美債所要求的風險溢酬。低於 400bps 為風險偏好，高於 600bps 為壓力，超過 1000bps 為衰退級恐慌。是信用循環轉折的領先指標。

indicators.desc_en: Fed's broad trade-weighted dollar index against major trading partners' currencies. A strong dollar pressures multinational earnings, commodity prices (negatively), and emerging market debt. A weakening dollar typically boosts US equities and EM assets.
indicators.desc_zh: 聯準會編製的貿易加權廣義美元指數，對美國主要貿易夥伴貨幣加權。強勢美元壓縮跨國企業獲利、商品價格與新興市場債務；弱勢美元通常利多美股與新興市場資產。

indicators.desc_en: West Texas Intermediate crude oil spot price per barrel. Oil prices feed into inflation, transportation costs, and consumer discretionary spending. Sustained moves above $90 typically pressure CPI; below $60 raises concerns about global demand.
indicators.desc_zh: 西德州中級原油現貨價格（每桶美元）。油價影響通膨、運輸成本與消費者可支配支出。持續高於 90 美元常推高 CPI；低於 60 美元則引發全球需求疑慮。

indicators.desc_en: Broad measure of US money supply including currency, deposits, and money market funds. M2 YoY growth correlates with inflation 12-18 months ahead. Post-2022 contraction was the first since the Great Depression, signaling tight liquidity.
indicators.desc_zh: 美國廣義貨幣供給，含現金、存款與貨幣市場基金。M2 年增率領先通膨 12-18 個月。2022 年後的萎縮是自大蕭條以來首見，反映流動性緊縮。

indicators.desc_en: Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey from BLS—total unfilled job postings. The job openings to unemployed ratio (currently watched closely by Powell) signals labor market tightness. A drop below 1.0 typically precedes wage disinflation.
indicators.desc_zh: 勞工部 JOLTS 調查的未填補職位總數。職位空缺對失業人數比率（鮑爾密切關注的指標）反映勞動市場緊俏程度，低於 1.0 通常領先工資通膨降溫。

indicators.desc_en: Average hourly earnings of all private-sector employees. YoY growth above 4% is consistent with sticky 2%+ services inflation. Fed watches wage growth as the lynchpin of services inflation dynamics.
indicators.desc_zh: 民間部門全體勞工平均時薪。年增率高於 4% 與黏性 2%+ 服務業通膨一致。聯準會將工資成長視為服務業通膨動態的關鍵指標。

indicators.desc_en: Percentage of industrial capacity actually in use. Above 80% indicates tight capacity (inflationary pressure); below 75% signals slack (deflationary). Recession averages historically run 73-75%.
indicators.desc_zh: 工業產能實際使用比例。高於 80% 代表產能吃緊（通膨壓力），低於 75% 代表閒置（通縮壓力）。歷史衰退期平均落在 73-75%。

indicators.name_zh: 芝加哥聯儲全國活動指數 CFNAI
indicators.desc_en: A weighted average of 85 monthly indicators of US national economic activity. Zero equals trend growth; the 3-month moving average below −0.7 has historically signaled the onset of recession, while readings above +0.7 (after an expansion) point to rising inflation pressure.
indicators.desc_zh: 由 85 項全美經濟活動月指標加權而成。0 代表趨勢成長；三個月移動平均低於 −0.7 歷史上預示衰退開始，高於 +0.7（在擴張期後）則暗示通膨壓力升高。

indicators.desc_en: Difference between US exports and imports of goods/services. Persistent deficits (currently ~$70B/month) pressure the dollar long-term. Sudden widening can signal domestic demand strength or import surges (often China-related).
indicators.desc_zh: 美國商品與服務出口減進口的差額。持續赤字（目前約每月 700 億美元）長期施壓美元。突然擴大可能反映內需強勁或進口激增（常與中國貿易相關）。

indicators.desc_en: BLS import price index covering all imported goods. A leading indicator for goods CPI—rising import prices typically pass through to consumer inflation within 1-3 quarters. Closely watched during trade-tension episodes.
indicators.desc_zh: 勞工部編製的全進口商品價格指數，是商品 CPI 的領先指標。進口物價上升通常 1-3 季後轉嫁至消費通膨，在貿易摩擦時期備受關注。

commentary.zh: 最新CPI指數達333.979，環比上漲0.5%，反映物價持續上升壓力。自2025年5月以來，指數累計上漲4.2%，顯示通膨並未有效回落。近期月度增速雖保持溫和，但3月至5月的加速態勢（3月躍升2.9%）令人擔憂。若年增率持續超過2%目標，聯準會可能需要維持較高利率水準，對經濟成長構成風險。市場應密切關注能源及服務類價格動向。
commentary.en: Latest CPI reading of 333.979 marks a 0.5% monthly increase, signaling persistent inflation pressures. Since May 2025, the index has climbed 4.2%, suggesting inflation remains sticky above the Fed's 2% target. The sharp 2.9% jump in March followed by sustained monthly gains raises concerns about underlying price momentum. If year-over-year inflation persists above target levels, the Fed may face pressure to maintain restrictive policy, potentially constraining growth. Investors should monitor energy and services inflation closely.

ai.zh: CPI持續攀升至333.979(月增0.5%)反映通膨壓力仍在,可能促使聯準會維持緊縮立場。對AI產業而言,高利率環境增加超大規模業者的資本成本,可能延緩部分資料中心擴建計畫。然而AI基礎建設投資具策略必要性,NVIDIA等晶片需求雖成長速度可能趨緩,但絕對水準仍維持高檔,企業透過AI提升效率的誘因反而因成本壓力更顯迫切。
ai.en: CPI rising to 333.979 (+0.5% MoM) sustains inflationary pressure, likely keeping Fed policy restrictive and raising capital costs for hyperscaler capex. This may slow marginal data center expansion timing at Microsoft/Google. However, AI infrastructure remains strategically essential—NVIDIA GPU demand stays elevated in absolute terms as enterprises accelerate AI adoption to offset rising operating costs through productivity gains. Net impact: moderated growth pace but sustained high investment baseline.

mobile.zh: CPI月增0.5%至333.979顯示生活成本持續上揚,直接壓縮消費者可支配所得。智慧型手機屬非必需性消費,換機週期將進一步延長。Apple與Samsung面臨需求疲軟,尤其中低階機型銷售承壓。消費者優先支付食品、能源等剛性支出,旗艦機種ASP提升策略效果受限。零組件庫存去化速度放緩,供應鏈訂單能見度轉弱,第三季出貨預期下修。
mobile.en: CPI climbing to 333.979 (+0.5% MoM) squeezes disposable income as essential goods costs rise. Smartphone demand—a discretionary category—faces headwinds with extended replacement cycles. Apple and Samsung shipments soften, particularly in mid-range segments, as consumers prioritize necessities over electronics. Premium ASP strategies lose traction. Component inventory digestion slows, reducing supply chain order visibility and pressuring Q3 shipment forecasts. Consumer electronics retail traffic weakens amid persistent inflation.

auto.zh: CPI升至333.979且月增0.5%,通膨居高不下將使聯準會維持高利率,直接推升汽車貸款成本。新車月付款已處歷史高位,進一步抑制購車需求,尤其影響需要融資的中產消費者。電動車雖享補貼但價格仍高,採用速度放緩。傳統車廠庫存天數上升,促銷壓力加大侵蝕利潤。供應鏈端MCU、功率元件訂單能見度下降,汽車半導體需求轉弱。
auto.en: CPI at 333.979 (+0.5% MoM) keeps Fed rates elevated, directly raising auto loan costs when monthly payments already hit records. This suppresses vehicle demand, especially among middle-income buyers reliant on financing. EV adoption slows despite incentives due to high upfront costs. OEM inventory days rise, forcing margin-eroding incentives. Dealer traffic weakens. Supply chain impact: MCU and power semiconductor orders lose visibility as automakers trim production forecasts, reducing wafer content per vehicle and component demand.

tsmc.zh: CPI月增0.5%達333.979,通膨壓力下客戶群呈現分化。AI/HPC客戶(NVIDIA、AMD)的3nm/5nm先進製程需求維持強勁,CoWoS產能持續滿載。然而智慧型手機(Apple、高通)與車用晶片需求轉弱,成熟製程稼動率面臨下修壓力。整體而言先進製程撐住營收與毛利率,但7nm以上製程利用率下滑。資本支出維持但結構轉向EUV設備,反映客戶組合朝高階AI晶片傾斜。
tsmc.en: CPI rising to 333.979 (+0.5% MoM) creates bifurcated wafer demand at TSMC. AI/HPC customers (NVIDIA, AMD) sustain robust 3nm/5nm orders with CoWoS capacity fully booked, supporting revenue and margins. However, smartphone (Apple A-series, Qualcomm) and automotive chip demand weakens amid consumer spending pressure, lowering mature node utilization rates. Advanced nodes remain resilient but 7nm+ capacity softens. Capex outlook steady but skewed toward EUV tools, reflecting customer mix shift toward high-margin AI accelerators over consumer electronics.

commentary.zh: 5月核心CPI達336.121，月增0.2%，延續溫和上升趨勢。自去年5月以來累計上漲2.8%，顯示通膨雖未加速但仍具韌性。近12個月漲幅穩定在0.2%左右的月度水準，反映供給面壓力持續。聯準會關注焦點在於通膨是否朝2%目標收斂，目前數據暗示降息時程可能延後。市場應密切觀察後續月份數據，若升幅超過0.3%將強化Fed維持高利率立場。
commentary.en: May core CPI reached 336.121, rising 0.2% month-over-month, sustaining a gradual uptrend. Year-over-year gain of 2.8% since May 2025 signals persistent inflation resilience despite lack of acceleration. Monthly increases averaging 0.2% suggest underlying price pressures remain entrenched. The Fed's path to rate cuts depends on further disinflation evidence. Markets should monitor upcoming prints closely—readings above 0.3% monthly would reinforce pause expectations and delay easing cycles.

ai.zh: 核心CPI月增0.2%至336.121，年化約2.4%的通膨仍高於聯準會目標，降息預期延後。這壓抑AI資本支出的融資成本，但數據中心電力與冷卻設備成本持續攀升，侵蝕營運利潤率。NVIDIA、Microsoft等大型雲端業者的GPU採購計畫可能延後或分階段執行，以平衡資本配置與股東回報壓力，短期資本支出增速放緩。
ai.en: Core CPI up 0.2% to 336.121 implies ~2.4% annualized inflation, keeping Fed rates higher for longer. This raises financing costs for AI capex, while data center operating expenses (power, cooling) continue climbing, compressing margins. Hyperscalers like Microsoft and Google may phase GPU purchases more cautiously to balance capex intensity against shareholder returns. NVIDIA's near-term order momentum faces headwinds as customers optimize spending amid sustained elevated borrowing costs.

mobile.zh: 核心CPI連續12個月上漲至336.121，累積漲幅2.8%削弱消費者購買力，尤其打擊1000美元以上高階手機需求。Apple與Samsung的旗艦機換機週期拉長至3-4年，分期付款利率上升使月付金額增加15-20%。服務類通膨（住房、醫療）擠壓可支配所得，消費者轉向中階機種或延後升級，2026下半年出貨量恐年減5-7%。
mobile.en: Core CPI's 12-month climb to 336.121 (up 2.8% cumulatively) erodes discretionary spending, particularly for $1,000+ flagship smartphones. Apple and Samsung face extended replacement cycles (3-4 years) as installment financing costs rise 15-20%, pushing consumers toward mid-tier models. Service inflation (shelter, healthcare) squeezes disposable income, delaying upgrades. Shipment volumes likely contract 5-7% YoY in H2 2026 as affordability pressures intensify despite promotional efforts.

auto.zh: 核心CPI月增0.2%強化聯準會鷹派立場，汽車貸款利率維持7-8%高位，使月供成本較2023年增加25-30%。皮卡與SUV需求疲軟，庫存天數升至75天以上。EV補貼縮減疊加充電基礎建設成本上漲，電動車滲透率成長停滯於18%。供應鏈去庫存延續，車用MCU與功率半導體需求年減10-12%，OEM削減2026下半年排產計畫。
auto.en: Core CPI rising 0.2% to 336.121 keeps auto loan rates at 7-8%, lifting monthly payments 25-30% versus 2023. Pickup and SUV demand softens, pushing dealer inventory to 75+ days. EV adoption stalls near 18% as subsidy reductions and charging infrastructure inflation bite. Supply chain destocking persists; automotive MCU and power semiconductor demand drops 10-12% YoY. OEMs cut H2 2026 production schedules, pressuring semiconductor content per vehicle and utilization rates.

tsmc.zh: 核心CPI年化2.4%延後降息，強勢美元使TSMC新台幣計價成本相對降低，但客戶端資本支出趨謹慎。AI訓練晶片（CoWoS先進封裝）需求穩健維持3nm/5nm產能利用率90%以上，但手機與車用晶片疲軟拖累7nm以上成熟製程至75%。客戶組合分化明顯：雲端GPU訂單支撐先進製程，消費電子與車用客戶庫存調整延長至2026Q3，整體晶圓出貨量年增放緩至個位數。
tsmc.en: Core CPI at 336.121 (annualized 2.4%) delays rate cuts, strengthening the USD and lowering TSMC's TWD-denominated costs, yet customer capex grows cautious. AI training chips (CoWoS advanced packaging) sustain 3nm/5nm utilization above 90%, but smartphone and auto weakness drags 7nm+ nodes to 75%. Customer mix diverges: hyperscaler GPU orders support leading-edge, while consumer electronics and automotive clients extend inventory correction into Q3 2026. Overall wafer shipment growth decelerates to low-single digits YoY.

commentary.zh: 4月核心PCE升至129.63，月增0.2%，延續溫和上升趨勢。自去年5月的125.79以來，已累計上漲3.84點（3.1%），顯示通膨壓力仍未完全消退。雖然近月漲幅已放緩至0.2-0.3%區間，但年化速度仍高於聯準會2%目標。此數據可能支持聯準會維持審慎態度，暗示降息時程可能延後。市場應留意後續數據是否能確認通膨持續回落趨勢。
commentary.en: Core PCE rose to 129.63 in April, up 0.2% monthly, extending a modest upward trajectory. Since May 2025's 125.79, the index has climbed 3.84 points (3.1%), signaling persistent inflation pressures. While recent monthly gains have moderated to 0.2-0.3%, the annualized pace still exceeds the Fed's 2% target. This reading likely reinforces the Fed's cautious stance, suggesting delayed rate-cut timing. Markets should monitor whether subsequent data confirms a sustained disinflationary trend.

ai.zh: 核心 PCE 達 129.63（月增 0.2%），年化通膨約 3.7%，持續高於聯準會 2% 目標。此壓力將延遲降息預期，維持高利率環境對 AI 資本支出形成壓力。然而超大規模業者（Microsoft、Google）將 AI 基礎設施視為戰略必需，GPU 與數據中心投資韌性強。NVIDIA H100/H200 訂單仍滿載，但估值面臨利率成本挑戰，資金成本上升可能延緩中小型 AI 新創的擴張速度。
ai.en: Core PCE at 129.63 (+0.2% MoM, ~3.7% annualized) remains well above the Fed's 2% target, postponing rate cuts and sustaining high borrowing costs that pressure AI capex financing. Hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google, Meta) treat AI infrastructure as strategic imperative, maintaining robust GPU orders and data center builds despite elevated rates. NVIDIA's H100/B200 demand remains strong, but higher discount rates compress valuations. Smaller AI startups face tighter venture funding and delayed expansions as cost of capital rises, creating bifurcation in AI investment momentum.

mobile.zh: 核心 PCE 連續 12 個月上升至 129.63，累積通膨侵蝕消費者可支配所得，直接打擊非必需消費電子支出。智慧型手機換機週期已延長至 3.5 年以上，持續通膨壓力將進一步推遲升級需求。Apple iPhone 平均售價超過 $1,000、Samsung 旗艦機亦逾 $900，在實質購買力下降環境中面臨降級消費風險。高通膨亦使聯準會維持緊縮立場，信用卡利率居高不下，分期付款成本上升抑制高價旗艦機型銷售動能。
mobile.en: Core PCE climbing to 129.63 (12-month uptrend) erodes real disposable income, directly pressuring discretionary consumer electronics spending. Smartphone replacement cycles already stretched beyond 3.5 years will extend further as persistent inflation (~3.7% annualized) squeezes budgets. Apple's $1,000+ iPhone ASPs and Samsung's $900+ flagships face downgrade risk as real purchasing power declines. Elevated inflation keeps Fed hawkish, sustaining high credit card rates (20%+) that increase installment costs, dampening premium flagship demand. Expect mix shift toward mid-range devices and softer unit shipments through H2 2026.

auto.zh: 核心 PCE 月增 0.2% 至 129.63，年化通膨率 3.7% 確保聯準會維持高利率政策。目前新車貸款利率已逾 7.5%、中古車貸超過 11%，持續通膨將阻止利率回落，進一步提高每月還款負擔。以 $48,000 新車計算，高利率環境使月付款增加 $150-200，直接抑制皮卡與 SUV 銷售。電動車方面，售價較高（平均 $55,000+）對利率更敏感，補貼退場疊加融資成本上升，削弱 EV 採用動能。車用晶片需求因銷量疲軟而放緩。
auto.en: Core PCE at 129.63 (+0.2% MoM, 3.7% annualized) keeps the Fed in restrictive mode, locking in auto loan rates above 7.5% for new vehicles and 11%+ for used. At $48,000 average new vehicle price, sustained high rates add $150-200 to monthly payments, directly crimping pickup and SUV demand. EV adoption faces dual headwinds: higher average prices ($55,000+) amplify rate sensitivity, while subsidy phase-outs and elevated financing costs slow conversion. Expect softer SAAR below 15.5M units, reducing automotive semiconductor content demand and delaying supply chain restocking through Q3 2026.

tsmc.zh: 核心 PCE 持續高於目標（129.63，年增 3.7%）延遲降息時程，對客戶資本支出形成壓力，但影響呈現結構分化。AI 需求支撐 N3/N4 先進製程滿載，NVIDIA、AMD 訂單維持強勁，CoWoS 封裝產能持續吃緊。然而消費電子疲軟（智慧型手機、PC）壓抑 N5/N7 利用率，Apple A 系列、M 系列處理器投片量因 iPhone/Mac 需求降溫而放緩。車用晶片（N7/N16）需求亦因高利率抑制汽車銷售而轉弱。整體而言先進製程韌性抵銷成熟製程壓力，但資本支出回報率面臨利率成本挑戰。
tsmc.en: Core PCE at 129.63 (3.7% annualized) sustaining high rates creates bifurcated impact on TSMC. AI-driven N3/N4 demand remains robust—NVIDIA H200/B200 and AMD MI300 orders keep advanced nodes at full utilization, with CoWoS capacity bottlenecked through 2026. However, consumer electronics weakness (smartphones, PCs) softens N5/N7 utilization as Apple reduces A-series and M-series wafer starts amid dampened iPhone/Mac demand. Automotive chips (N7/N16) face headwinds from rate-sensitive auto sales decline. Net effect: advanced node strength offsets mature node softness, but elevated discount rates pressure capex ROI on new Arizona/Japan fabs, potentially moderating 2027 expansion pace.

commentary.zh: 美國非農就業人數5月達159,001千人，環比增加172千人（+0.1%），延續溫和成長態勢。近12個月數據在158,400～159,000千間波動，顯示就業市場已進入平台期，遠低於健康水準的每月+150,000增速。這種停滯性成長暗示勞動力需求冷卻，可能反映經濟成長放緩信號。聯準會將密切關注此趨勢，若持續疲軟恐加速降息預期，對股市形成支撐但也顯示經濟韌性減弱。
commentary.en: Nonfarm payrolls reached 159,001k in May, rising only 172k month-over-month—a marginal 0.1% gain. Over 12 months, the metric has stalled between 158,400-159,000k, well below the 150k+ threshold signaling healthy labor demand. This plateau suggests cooling hiring momentum, likely reflecting softer economic growth. The Fed faces pressure to eventually ease policy if weakness persists. Markets may interpret this as dovish, supporting equities, yet it signals diminishing economic momentum and potential earnings risks ahead.

ai.zh: 非農就業增加17.2萬人，超越15萬健康門檻，顯示勞動市場穩健。強勁就業支撐企業獲利預期，促使超大規模雲端業者（微軟、Google、Meta）維持AI基礎設施資本支出計畫。數據中心建設需求持續，NVIDIA H100/H200及B200 GPU訂單能見度良好。就業穩定亦降低聯準會激進降息壓力，利率維持中性區間有利AI長期投資回報率計算，超大規模客戶資本配置更具確定性。
ai.en: +172K payroll gain exceeds the +150K health threshold, signaling robust labor market. Strong employment bolsters corporate profit outlook, sustaining hyperscaler (Microsoft, Google, AWS) AI capex commitments for data center buildouts. GPU demand from NVIDIA (H100/B200) remains solid with extended order visibility. Healthy job growth reduces Fed pressure for aggressive rate cuts, keeping rates in neutral range that supports favorable IRR calculations for multi-year AI infrastructure investments and stabilizes hyperscaler capital allocation budgets.

mobile.zh: 5月非農新增17.2萬人，就業年增率僅0.1%顯示增速放緩但未衰退。消費者信心維持但缺乏加速動能，對高階智慧手機（iPhone Pro系列、Galaxy S系列）換機週期影響中性。就業穩定支撐基本換機需求，但增幅有限難以推動平均售價提升或加速5G滲透。Apple及Samsung出貨量持平，庫存週期正常化。聯準會觀望態度下利率暫不降低，分期付款成本未改善，難刺激旗艦機種需求突破。
mobile.en: +172K May gain with only +0.1% year-over-year growth shows deceleration but avoids contraction. Consumer confidence remains stable yet lacks acceleration, creating neutral impact on premium smartphone replacement cycles (iPhone Pro, Galaxy S flagship). Steady employment supports baseline upgrade demand but insufficient momentum to lift ASPs or accelerate 5G adoption. Apple and Samsung shipments track sideways with normalizing inventory. Fed hold stance keeps financing costs unchanged—installment plan rates remain elevated, failing to catalyze flagship model demand breakthrough in a saturated upgrade market.

auto.zh: 非農增加17.2萬人提供就業支撐，但0.1%年增率顯示動能減弱。就業穩定維持汽車貸款信用品質，降低違約風險，有利經銷商融資環境。然而新增就業放緩抑制新購車需求擴張，SAAR（季調年化銷售率）難突破1,550萬輛。電動車方面，就業增長不足以抵消聯邦稅收抵免政策不確定性，Tesla及傳統車廠EV銷售增速趨緩。供應鏈MCU及車用晶片需求維持平穩但缺乏上行驅動，庫存回補週期接近尾聲。
auto.en: +172K addition provides employment stability, maintaining auto loan credit quality and lowering default risks for dealer financing. However, +0.1% annual growth signals weakening momentum—insufficient to expand new vehicle demand beyond ~15.5M SAAR. EV adoption faces headwinds as modest job gains cannot offset federal tax credit policy uncertainty, slowing Tesla and legacy OEM EV sales growth. Supply chain demand for MCUs and auto chips remains steady but lacks upside catalyst; inventory replenishment cycle nearing completion. Mixed outlook: credit risk contained, volume growth constrained.

tsmc.zh: 非農穩健增長鞏固美國終端需求，直接利好TSMC主要客戶群。AI晶片方面，超大規模客戶（NVIDIA、AMD、Google TPU）N3E及CoWoS先進封裝投片量維持滿載，3奈米利用率超過95%。手機端雖需求平穩，但Apple A18及高通Snapdragon 8 Gen 4於N3P製程訂單穩定Q3量產準備。汽車客戶（NVIDIA Drive、Mobileye）MCU及ADAS晶片需求持平。整體客戶組合健康，就業數據降低經濟硬著陸風險，支撐2026年資本支出維持280-320億美元區間，先進製程擴產計畫不變。
tsmc.en: Solid payroll print reinforces US end-demand, directly benefiting TSMC's key customer base. AI chip segment: hyperscaler clients (NVIDIA, AMD, Google TPU) sustain full N3E wafer loading and CoWoS advanced packaging, with 3nm utilization above 95%. Mobile remains stable—Apple A18 and Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Gen 4 on N3P node locked for Q3 ramp. Auto clients (NVIDIA Drive, Mobileye) show steady MCU and ADAS chip demand. Healthy customer mix and reduced hard-landing risk support capex guidance of $28-32B through 2026, keeping advanced node expansion on track.

commentary.zh: 最新失業率維持在4.3%，與前月持平。過去一年內波動介於4.1%至4.5%之間，整體呈現穩定態勢。雖略高於充分就業的3.5-4%目標區間，但仍處於合理水準。勞動市場未見明顯惡化跡象，維持相對韌性。此穩定表現為聯準會評估貨幣政策提供了靈活空間，支持其在通膨與就業間的平衡考量。市場應持續關注就業數據走勢，以判斷未來政策方向。
commentary.en: The unemployment rate holds steady at 4.3%, unchanged from the prior month. Over the past year, it has fluctuated between 4.1% and 4.5%, demonstrating stability. Though slightly above the full employment target of 3.5-4%, it signals a resilient labor market with no signs of deterioration. This steady performance affords the Fed flexibility in balancing its dual mandate of maximum employment and price stability. Markets should monitor ongoing employment trends to gauge future policy trajectories.

ai.zh: 失業率維持在4.3%，經過前期4.5%高點後持穩，顯示勞動市場溫和但未過熱。這對AI產業有利：企業因應穩定就業成本持續投資數據中心自動化與AI基礎設施，hyperscaler資本支出（Microsoft、Google）未受裁員壓力干擾。NVIDIA GPU需求保持強勁，因4.3%水平未觸發經濟衰退警訊，企業AI capex信心穩固。聯準會雙重任務平衡下，利率政策不至激進緊縮，支撐長期AI投資週期。
ai.en: Unemployment stable at 4.3% after peaking at 4.5% in November suggests a cooling but resilient labor market. This supports AI capex as hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google, Meta) face predictable wage costs without recessionary pressure, sustaining data center buildouts. NVIDIA GPU demand remains robust as 4.3% sits above full employment (3.5-4%) but avoids recession signals that would halt enterprise AI spending. The Fed's dual mandate balance implies no aggressive tightening, preserving favorable financing for multi-year AI infrastructure investments and advanced node chip orders.

mobile.zh: 失業率4.3%持平，接近充分就業但略高，對手機需求構成中性影響。消費者就業穩定支撐可支配收入，但4.3%非強勁擴張水平，抑制iPhone與Galaxy旗艦機型的升級週期加速。過去一年4.1%-4.5%窄幅波動顯示需求不冷不熱：Apple與Samsung出貨量維持溫和成長，但缺乏4%以下充分就業時的消費爆發力。消費電子替換需求穩定，高階機型銷售增長有限，中階市場競爭持續。
mobile.en: Unemployment holding at 4.3%, unchanged and near full employment, creates neutral smartphone demand dynamics. Stable jobs support discretionary income for Apple and Samsung, but 4.3% lacks the sub-4% tightness that historically drives premium upgrade cycles. The 4.1-4.5% range over 12 months signals steady but unspectacular consumer electronics replacement demand. Flagship iPhone and Galaxy shipments remain modest as consumers delay upgrades absent wage acceleration. Mid-tier Android competition intensifies as aspirational buyers remain cautious, keeping ASPs pressured and volume growth tepid.

auto.zh: 失業率4.3%不變，對汽車業呈現混合訊號。穩定就業支撐購車信心，但4.3%高於3.5-4%充分就業區間，薪資增長放緩削弱新車（尤其EV）購買力。汽車貸款利率因聯準會維持較高利率而昂貴，月付款壓力抑制銷量。皮卡與SUV需求溫和，但Tesla與傳統車廠EV採用受限於4.3%失業環境下消費者對高價車款謹慎。供應鏈穩定，但融資成本與就業前景不確定性限制2026上半年銷售動能。
auto.en: Unemployment flat at 4.3% presents mixed auto signals. Stable employment supports consumer confidence for vehicle purchases, but 4.3%—above the 3.5-4% full employment zone—indicates wage growth moderation that pressures affordability for new vehicles, especially $50k+ EVs. Auto loan rates remain elevated as the Fed balances its employment mandate, raising monthly payments and crimping pickup/SUV demand. Tesla and legacy OEM EV adoption faces headwinds as cautious buyers at 4.3% unemployment defer expensive electrification. Supply chains stabilize, yet financing costs and labor market uncertainty cap sales momentum through mid-2026.

tsmc.zh: 失業率4.3%持穩對台積電呈中性影響。穩定勞動市場維持AI晶片（NVIDIA H100/B200）與數據中心需求，3nm/5nm先進製程利用率保持健康。然而4.3%水平抑制智慧手機與消費電子復甦力道，Apple A系列與高通訂單成長受限。客戶組合分化：hyperscaler AI需求強（佔營收比重提升），但手機與車用晶片（7nm以上）需求平淡。資本支出展望審慎，因失業率未降至4%以下難以驅動全面消費復甦，台積電維持高階製程擴產但成熟製程投資保守。
tsmc.en: Unemployment steady at 4.3% yields neutral TSMC implications. Stable labor markets sustain AI chip demand (NVIDIA, AMD) for 3nm/5nm wafers, keeping advanced node utilization robust as hyperscaler capex continues. However, 4.3%—not tightening toward 4%—limits smartphone recovery, constraining Apple A-series and Qualcomm orders at mature nodes. Customer mix diverges: AI and data center wafer demand grows (higher revenue share), while mobile and auto chip orders (7nm+) stagnate. Capex outlook remains disciplined; without unemployment dropping below 4%, broad consumer end-market recovery is muted, prompting TSMC to prioritize leading-edge expansion but defer aggressive mature-node investment.

commentary.zh: 最新數據升至22.9萬，較前週增加4千人（+1.8%），延續近期上升趨勢。過去六週內，申請人數從19萬攀升至22.9萬，漲幅約20%。儘管仍遠低於30萬的警戒線，但上升動能值得關注。五月末以來連續三週超過21萬，顯示勞動市場冷卻跡象。四週移動平均仍維持在相對溫和水準，但若升勢持續，可能影響聯準會對就業市場強度的評估，進而影響降息時間表。
commentary.en: Latest reading of 229k marks a 1.8% increase, extending recent deterioration. Claims have risen roughly 20% over six weeks from 190k lows, with three consecutive weeks above 210k. While comfortably below the 300k recession-warning threshold, the trend warrants monitoring. The four-week moving average remains moderate, yet sustained increases could signal labor market cooling, potentially influencing Fed expectations on rate cuts and employment dynamics heading into mid-year.

ai.zh: 初次申領失業救濟金人數上升至22.9萬，較前週增加4千人，但仍遠低於30萬警戒線，顯示勞動市場依然穩健。企業資本支出信心維持，超大規模雲端業者（微軟、谷歌）的資料中心投資與GPU採購計劃不受影響。然而近期趨勢從4月低點19萬逐步攀升，需觀察是否預示企業開始謹慎控制成本，可能延緩2026下半年AI基礎設施擴張速度。輝達等供應商短期訂單能見度仍佳。
ai.en: Initial claims rose to 229K (+4K), still well below the 300K deterioration threshold, indicating labor market resilience that supports continued AI capex by hyperscalers. Microsoft, Google, and AWS data center investments and GPU procurement remain on track. However, the uptrend from April's 190K low warrants monitoring—sustained increases could signal corporate cost caution, potentially decelerating H2 2026 infrastructure expansion. NVIDIA and AI hardware suppliers maintain strong near-term order visibility, but gradual weakening may temper 2027 capacity additions.

mobile.zh: 失業救濟申領人數從5月初19.9萬攀升至22.9萬，月內增幅達15%，顯示消費者就業安全感邊際轉弱。智慧手機為可選消費品，換機週期對收入預期極為敏感。蘋果與三星面臨北美市場需求疲軟風險，尤其高階旗艦機型（iPhone Pro系列、Galaxy Ultra）銷售承壓。若申領人數持續向25萬推進，第三季返校季與第四季假期銷售旺季可能低於預期，迫使供應鏈下修零組件訂單並加劇庫存調整壓力。
mobile.en: Claims climbed 15% month-over-month from 199K to 229K, signaling weakening job security that pressures discretionary smartphone spending. Apple and Samsung face softer North American demand, particularly for high-tier models (iPhone Pro, Galaxy Ultra) where upgrade cycles are income-sensitive. If claims trend toward 250K, Q3 back-to-school and Q4 holiday seasons risk missing forecasts, forcing supply chain order cuts and inventory corrections. Consumer electronics retailers may accelerate promotional activity, compressing margins across the mobile ecosystem as replacement cycles extend further.

auto.zh: 失業救濟人數從4月低點19萬上升至當前22.9萬，六週累計增幅21%，削弱消費者購車信心。汽車貸款依賴穩定就業預期，申領人數攀升直接收緊信貸審批標準並提高違約風險溢價。傳統燃油車與電動車銷售均承壓，但電動車因單價較高更敏感—福特、通用的電動皮卡訂單可能流失。供應鏈端，晶片需求（MCU、車用GPU）轉弱，經銷商庫存天數上升，迫使車廠減產並延後新產能投資。
auto.en: Claims surged 21% over six weeks from 190K to 229K, eroding consumer confidence critical for auto purchases requiring multi-year financing. Lenders tighten credit standards as unemployment risk rises, increasing loan rejection rates and interest rate premiums. EV sales face disproportionate pressure due to higher price points—Ford and GM electric truck orders may soften. Auto suppliers see weakening demand for MCUs and ADAS chips as dealership inventory days climb, prompting OEMs to curtail production schedules and defer capacity expansions, particularly for EV-dedicated platforms.

tsmc.zh: 失業救濟人數上升至22.9萬反映消費端轉弱，直接衝擊智慧手機與汽車晶片需求—台積電N3與N4製程的蘋果A系列與高通訂單可能下修，車用MCU（N16/N28）稼動率面臨壓力。然而AI伺服器晶片（輝達H100/B200使用N4P/N3E）需求由企業資本支出驅動，目前不受消費疲軟影響，先進製程利用率仍維持高檔。客戶組合風險上升：消費電子占比下降由AI/HPC補位，但若就業數據持續惡化可能延遲2027年擴產時程。
tsmc.en: Rising claims to 229K pressures consumer-facing chip demand—Apple A-series and Qualcomm smartphone processors on N3/N4 face potential order cuts, while automotive MCUs (N16/N28) see utilization headwinds. However, AI server chips (NVIDIA H100/B200 on N4P/N3E) driven by enterprise capex remain insulated, keeping advanced node loading robust. Customer mix shifts as consumer electronics share declines, offset by AI/HPC growth. If claims breach 250K sustainably, TSMC may moderate 2027 capex expansion, though near-term wafer pricing power persists on tight leading-edge capacity.

commentary.zh: 美國實質GDP於2026年1月達24152.656億美元，環比成長0.4%，延續溫和擴張態勢。自2023年4月以來累計成長7.0%，年化成長約2.3%，符合長期健康水準。近兩年未現技術性衰退風險，惟成長動能趨緩——2025年下半年季度環比增速徘徊0.3-0.4%，低於2024年同期。通膨調整後的經濟表現穩定，但放緩跡象明顯。聯準會政策調整空間取決於後續通膨數據，市場應密切監測勞動市場強度與消費支出動向。
commentary.en: Real GDP reached $24,152.7 billion in Q1 2026, growing 0.4% sequentially and maintaining steady expansion. Since April 2023, cumulative growth stands at 7.0%, translating to roughly 2.3% annualized—within healthy ranges. No recession signals emerge, yet momentum has decelerated noticeably; recent quarters show 0.3-0.4% sequential growth, below 2024 levels. The economy remains resilient but moderating. Fed policy flexibility depends on inflation trajectory; markets should monitor labor market health and consumer spending patterns closely.

ai.zh: 實質GDP達24,152億美元，季增0.4%，顯示經濟穩健擴張。此溫和成長支撐企業IT支出預算，有利微軟、Google等超大規模業者持續資料中心投資。NVIDIA GPU需求維持強勁，因企業在非衰退環境下更願意進行長期AI基礎建設資本支出。然增速放緩至0.4%意味成長動能減弱，可能促使部分客戶延後非關鍵性AI專案，但核心訓練需求仍穩固。
ai.en: Real GDP reached $24,152.7B with modest +0.4% QoQ growth, indicating stable expansion that sustains corporate IT budgets. This supports continued data center capex by hyperscalers like Microsoft and Google, keeping NVIDIA GPU demand robust as non-recessionary conditions justify long-term AI infrastructure investments. However, the deceleration to +0.4% from prior quarters suggests moderating momentum, potentially causing some enterprises to defer non-critical AI projects while core training workloads remain firm.

mobile.zh: GDP季增0.4%反映消費動能放緩，對智慧型手機等非必需消費品構成逆風。歷史數據顯示2025年Q1曾出現負成長(-0.2%)後反彈，當前微幅增長難以刺激換機需求顯著回升。蘋果與三星在成熟市場面臨ASP壓力，因消費者在經濟不確定下傾向延長使用週期。但AI功能手機可能吸引高端用戶，抵銷部分需求疲軟，整體出貨量預計持平或微增。
mobile.en: GDP's +0.4% growth reflects slowing consumer momentum, creating headwinds for discretionary purchases like smartphones. Historical data shows Q1 2025 contraction (-0.2%) followed by recovery; current tepid growth unlikely to trigger strong replacement cycles. Apple and Samsung face ASP pressure in mature markets as consumers extend device lifecycles amid economic uncertainty. However, AI-enabled features may attract premium segment buyers, partially offsetting weakness. Overall shipments expected flat to marginally positive as economic expansion remains fragile.

auto.zh: GDP增長0.4%低於健康水平2-3%，削弱汽車銷售動能。若聯準會因經濟放緩而維持利率，汽車貸款成本仍高，抑制皮卡與SUV等高價車款需求。電動車方面，補貼政策在財政壓力下存不確定性，影響消費者購買意願。然溫和正成長避免技術性衰退，防止車市崩盤，商用車隊對電動化的投資仍在進行。供應鏈庫存正常化，但MCU與車用晶片需求因銷量疲軟而趨緩。
auto.en: GDP growth at +0.4% falls short of the healthy 2-3% range, undermining auto sales momentum. If the Fed maintains elevated rates due to sluggish growth, auto loan costs remain prohibitive, suppressing demand for high-ticket trucks and SUVs. EV adoption faces uncertainty as fiscal pressures threaten subsidies, dampening consumer incentives. However, positive growth averts technical recession, preventing market collapse; commercial fleet electrification continues. Supply chains normalized, but MCU and automotive chip demand softens due to weak unit sales, reducing semiconductor content growth despite vehicle feature complexity.

tsmc.zh: GDP季增0.4%顯示全球最大經濟體擴張趨緩，對台積電影響分歧。AI晶片（3奈米/5奈米）需求因超大規模業者持續投資而保持強勁，先進製程利用率維持高檔。然智慧型手機與汽車晶片需求疲軟，7奈米以上成熟製程面臨去化壓力。客戶組合中NVIDIA、AMD貢獻提升，但蘋果iPhone處理器訂單可能因換機週期延長而低於預期。整體晶圓需求呈結構性分化，資本支出outlook審慎，聚焦高毛利先進製程。
tsmc.en: GDP's +0.4% growth signals slowing expansion in the world's largest economy, creating mixed effects for TSMC. AI chip demand (3nm/5nm) remains strong as hyperscalers sustain capex, keeping advanced node utilization elevated. However, smartphone and auto chip demand softens, pressuring 7nm+ mature node capacity. Customer mix shifts toward NVIDIA and AMD contributions rising, while Apple iPhone processor orders may undershoot due to extended replacement cycles. Overall wafer demand shows structural divergence; capex outlook cautious, prioritizing high-margin leading-edge nodes over capacity expansion in mature processes.

commentary.zh: 2026年4月零售銷售（不含汽車）達656,115百萬美元，環比增長0.5%，延續穩健上升軌跡。自5月低點618,037以來，已累計成長6.2%，顯示消費動能持續強化。近三個月加速擴張（2月至4月月增率均在0.5%以上），印證美國經濟韌性。鑒於消費支出佔GDP七成，此數據暗示通膨壓力可能升溫，對聯準會降息預期構成挑戰。市場應密切關注後續通膨數據，以評估寬鬆政策延續的空間。
commentary.en: April ex-auto retail sales reached $656.1 billion, up 0.5% MoM, extending a steady uptrend since May 2025's $618.0 billion trough—a 6.2% cumulative gain. Acceleration intensified over the past three months, signaling robust consumer demand. Given consumption's 70% GDP weight, sustained growth raises inflation concerns, complicating Fed's policy path. While resilient retail activity supports economic momentum, it may temper near-term rate-cut expectations if coupled with price pressures. Monitor CPI data closely for policy implications.

ai.zh: 零售銷售額達6,561億美元，較上月增長0.5%，且自3月起加速成長（3月增1.87%，4月再增0.5%），顯示消費者支出強勁。這增強企業對AI基礎設施投資的信心，因零售數據驅動需求預測、庫存優化等AI應用需求。強勁消費支撐雲端服務營收成長，促使微軟、Google等超大規模業者持續擴增資料中心，NVIDIA GPU及AI加速器需求維持高檔。
ai.en: Retail sales reached $656.1B, up 0.5% month-over-month with accelerating momentum since March (+1.87% in March, +0.5% in April). Strong consumer spending validates enterprise investment in AI infrastructure for demand forecasting and inventory optimization. Robust retail activity drives cloud services revenue growth, sustaining hyperscaler capex from Microsoft, Google on data center expansion. This maintains elevated demand for NVIDIA GPUs and AI accelerators as retailers digitize operations.

mobile.zh: 零售銷售月增0.5%至6,561億美元，但自去年10月的6,313億以來僅成長3.9%（七個月），顯示消費動能溫和。手機為高單價非必需品，換機週期已延長至3-4年，消費者在通膨壓力下優先購買日常用品。雖4月數據穩定，但缺乏足以刺激大規模換機潮的爆發力，Apple與Samsung旗艦機種銷售可能持平，中低階機型相對抗跌。
mobile.en: Retail sales grew 0.5% to $656.1B but only +3.9% over seven months since October's $631.3B, indicating tepid momentum. Smartphones are discretionary big-ticket items; upgrade cycles have stretched to 3-4 years as consumers prioritize essentials amid inflation. While April data is stable, it lacks the surge needed to trigger mass replacement cycles. Apple and Samsung flagship sales likely remain flat; mid-to-low tier models show relative resilience as consumers trade down.

auto.zh: 本指標刨除汽車銷售，但整體零售額月增0.5%反映消費者財務狀況穩定，理論上有助汽車融資能力。然而自10月以來七個月僅增3.9%的緩慢成長，暗示可支配所得增長有限。高利率環境下汽車貸款成本仍高（平均利率約7-8%），抑制新車尤其電動車購買意願。供應鏈已正常化，但需求端缺乏強勁催化劑，銷量與車用晶片需求持平。
auto.en: While this metric excludes autos, the 0.5% monthly gain suggests stable consumer finances supporting auto financing capability. However, only +3.9% growth over seven months since October signals constrained disposable income expansion. With auto loan rates at 7-8%, financing costs remain prohibitive for new vehicle purchases, especially EVs. Supply chains have normalized but demand lacks strong catalysts. Expect flat auto sales and steady-but-unexciting semiconductor content demand for MCUs and ADAS chips.

tsmc.zh: 零售銷售連續兩月加速（3月+1.87%、4月+0.5%）達6,561億美元，驅動多重晶圓需求。強勁零售支撐雲端業者資本支出，帶動5奈米/3奈米先進製程訂單（AI加速器、伺服器CPU）。消費性電子雖溫和但穩定，維持7奈米/5奈米手機晶片基本負載。零售數據健康確保TSMC客戶組合平衡：超大規模業者AI晶片（高毛利）與消費電子（量穩），先進製程產能利用率維持85%以上高檔。
tsmc.en: Retail sales accelerated for two consecutive months (March +1.87%, April +0.5%) to $656.1B, driving multi-vector wafer demand. Strong retail supports hyperscaler capex, boosting 5nm/3nm orders for AI accelerators and server CPUs. Consumer electronics, though modest, remain stable and sustain baseline 7nm/5nm smartphone chip load. Healthy retail validates TSMC's balanced customer mix: hyperscaler AI chips (high-margin) plus consumer electronics (volume-stable). Advanced node utilization stays above 85%, supporting aggressive capex outlook for 2nm ramp.

commentary.zh: 聯邦基金利率自2025年6月的4.33%持續下降至當前3.63%，共下調70個基點，反映聯準會採取循序漸進的寬鬆政策。自1月起維持在3.64%附近，表明利率已趨於穩定。最新月度下跌1個基點顯示調整已近尾聲。此輪降息週期自秋季加速，可能對應通膨壓力緩解或經濟成長放緩。市場應注意聯準會是否進一步調整政策立場，以及低利率環境對股債及信用市場的長期影響。
commentary.en: The Federal Funds Rate has declined 70 basis points from 4.33% in June 2025 to the current 3.63%, reflecting the Fed's gradual easing cycle. The rate has stabilized near 3.64% since January, with this month's 1 basis point decline suggesting the adjustment phase is moderating. The acceleration of cuts from autumn onward likely reflects improving inflation dynamics or economic softening. Market participants should monitor whether the Fed signals further adjustments and assess implications of this accommodative stance on equities, bonds, and credit spreads.

ai.zh: 聯邦基金利率已從6月的4.33%降至5月的3.63%,累計降息70個基點,顯著降低超大規模業者的資本成本。NVIDIA、Microsoft、Google等企業的百億美元數據中心投資變得更具吸引力,債券融資成本下降直接提升AI基礎設施擴張的內部報酬率。較低利率環境支撐GPU需求持續強勁,尤其是訓練集群與推論加速器的大規模部署,有利AI資本支出週期延續。
ai.en: The Fed Funds rate has declined 70 bps from 4.33% in June to 3.63% in May, materially reducing capital costs for hyperscalers. This makes multibillion-dollar data center investments by NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Google more attractive as debt financing costs fall, improving internal returns on AI infrastructure buildouts. Lower rates support sustained GPU demand, particularly for large-scale training clusters and inference accelerators, extending the AI capex cycle as borrowing conditions ease for both equipment purchases and facility construction.

mobile.zh: 利率降至3.63%後消費者融資成本減輕,理論上有助高價旗艦機需求,尤其是iPhone Pro系列與Galaxy Ultra等1,000美元以上機型的分期付款負擔降低。然而近期降息速度已放緩(過去四個月持平於3.64%附近),邊際刺激效果遞減。換機週期延長至3-4年的結構性趨勢未因溫和降息而逆轉,Apple與Samsung出貨量僅能維持平穩,缺乏顯著成長動能。
mobile.en: The rate at 3.63% reduces consumer financing costs, theoretically helping premium smartphone demand—installment burdens ease for $1,000+ devices like iPhone Pro and Galaxy Ultra. However, the easing pace has stalled (flat at ~3.64% for four months), diminishing marginal stimulus. The structural shift toward 3-4 year replacement cycles persists despite modest rate cuts; Apple and Samsung shipments remain stable but lack strong growth catalysts, as lower rates haven't reversed the maturity of smartphone penetration and incremental innovation.

auto.zh: 聯邦基金利率從4.33%降至3.63%直接壓低汽車貸款利率,五年期新車貸款APR已從高峰回落,每月還款負擔減輕刺激皮卡與SUV需求回溫。電動車方面,降息改善Tesla、Rivian等業者的資本密集型工廠擴建財務可行性,同時降低消費者對35,000美元以上EV的融資門檻。然而供應鏈端MCU與功率半導體需求復甦仍需3-6個月傳導,短期庫存調整壓力猶存。
auto.en: The Fed Funds drop from 4.33% to 3.63% directly lowers auto loan rates; five-year new vehicle APRs have retreated from peaks, reducing monthly payment burdens and reviving pickup and SUV demand. For EVs, lower rates improve financial viability of capital-intensive factory expansions by Tesla and Rivian, while easing consumer financing barriers for $35,000+ vehicles. However, supply-chain demand for MCUs and power semiconductors needs 3-6 months to transmit; near-term inventory adjustments still create headwinds for component suppliers despite improving end-market financing conditions.

tsmc.zh: 利率降至3.63%對台積電形成三重利多:一、客戶(NVIDIA、AMD、Apple)的資本支出意願增強,3奈米與5奈米晶圓需求更穩固;二、台積電自身百億美元等級的亞利桑那與日本廠投資的借款成本下降,改善新廠IRR;三、智慧型手機與車用客戶終端需求回穩,帶動16/28奈米成熟製程稼動率於下半年回升。客戶組合中AI加速器佔比持續擴大,抵銷消費性產品的週期性波動。
tsmc.en: The 3.63% rate creates a triple tailwind for TSMC: first, customers like NVIDIA, AMD, and Apple gain cheaper capital for equipment purchases, solidifying 3nm and 5nm wafer demand; second, TSMC's own multibillion-dollar Arizona and Japan fab investments face lower borrowing costs, improving project IRRs; third, stabilizing smartphone and automotive end-demand lifts 16/28nm mature-node utilization in H2. The customer mix increasingly tilts toward AI accelerators, which buffer cyclical swings in consumer products. Advanced node economics strengthen as hyperscaler capex remains robust under easing monetary conditions.

commentary.zh: 30年期房貸利率升至6.52%，較前週上升4個基點。近六週呈上升趨勢，從4月23日低點6.23%攀升至目前水準，漲幅29個基點。雖未突破7%的臨界點，但持續上升反映市場對聯準會降息步伐的預期調整。房貸成本上升將進一步壓縮購房可負擔性，可能抑制成交量。後續關注通膨數據和聯準會政策信號，若利率維持高檔，房產市場成交動能恐面臨挑戰。
commentary.en: The 30-year mortgage rate rose to 6.52%, up 4 basis points week-over-week. The six-week uptrend reflects a 29 bp increase from the April 23 low of 6.23%, signaling market repricing of Fed rate-cut expectations. While below the 7% affordability threshold, the sustained climb pressures home buyer purchasing power and could dampen transaction volumes. Monitor upcoming inflation data and Fed communications closely—sustained higher rates risk further cooling housing demand despite remaining below critical levels.

ai.zh: 30年期房貸利率自4月低點6.23%回升至6.52%，但仍低於7%緊縮門檻。房貸利率上升反映長期利率預期，可能小幅推升超大規模業者債務融資成本，但AI基礎設施投資(如NVIDIA H100/B200採購、Microsoft/Google資料中心擴建)主要由營運現金流及戰略必要性驅動，對利率敏感度有限。當前利率水準對AI資本支出影響輕微。
ai.en: The 30-year mortgage rate climbed from April's 6.23% low to 6.52%, still below the 7% affordability threshold. Rising rates signal higher long-term borrowing costs for hyperscaler debt issuance, but AI infrastructure capex (NVIDIA GPU orders, Microsoft/Google data center builds) remains cash-flow funded and strategically imperative. Current rate levels pose minimal constraint on AI spending; hyperscalers prioritize competitive positioning over marginal financing costs in the generative AI race.

mobile.zh: 房貸利率自6.23%攀升至6.52%(+29個基點)壓縮家庭可支配所得，尤其打擊購屋族群的非必要消費。歷史顯示房貸成本上升導致消費者延後高價手機升級，偏好中階機型或延長換機週期至36個月以上。Apple與Samsung旗艦機(iPhone Pro系列、Galaxy S系列)需求面臨逆風，尤其美國市場佔Apple營收40%以上，利率回升趨勢將削弱高階機種銷售動能。
mobile.en: Mortgage rates rising 29 basis points from 6.23% to 6.52% squeeze discretionary spending for homebuying households. Historical patterns show elevated housing costs push consumers to delay premium smartphone upgrades, favor mid-tier models, and extend replacement cycles beyond 36 months. Apple and Samsung flagship devices (iPhone Pro, Galaxy S) face headwinds, particularly as the U.S. represents over 40% of Apple revenue. The upward rate trajectory threatens high-end smartphone sell-through momentum.

auto.zh: 房貸利率上升至6.52%直接連動汽車貸款成本(通常房貸利率+1~2%),新車貸款利率逼近8%區間，顯著削弱購車負擔能力。購屋者面臨每月房貸增加，壓縮購車預算，尤其打擊需長期貸款的電動車銷售(平均成交價$55,000+)。Ford、GM電動皮卡與Tesla需求承壓，經銷商庫存天數可能攀升，迫使車廠減少MCU、ADAS晶片訂單，供應鏈稼動率下滑。
auto.en: The 6.52% mortgage rate directly elevates auto loan costs (typically mortgage rate +1-2%), pushing new vehicle financing near 8%. Homebuyers facing higher monthly payments curtail auto budgets, particularly impacting EV sales requiring extended financing (average transaction price $55,000+). Ford, GM electric trucks and Tesla demand face pressure; rising dealer inventory days will force OEMs to trim MCU and ADAS chip orders, reducing supply chain utilization and semiconductor content per vehicle.

tsmc.zh: 房貸利率回升至6.52%對TSMC呈現分化影響。負面：消費性晶片需求疲軟，手機(Apple A系列、高通)與車用(NXP、Infineon)7奈米及以上成熟製程投片量承壓，整體產能利用率面臨下修風險。正面：AI資料中心需求持續強勁，NVIDIA H100/B200採用的4奈米/3奈米CoWoS先進封裝訂單不受利率影響，支撐高毛利製程滿載。客戶組合持續向AI傾斜，部分抵銷消費端疲弱。
tsmc.en: The 6.52% rate creates mixed effects for TSMC. Negative: consumer chip demand weakens as smartphone (Apple A-series, Qualcomm) and auto (NXP, Infineon) orders on 7nm and mature nodes face pressure, risking overall capacity utilization downgrades. Positive: AI datacenter demand remains robust; NVIDIA H100/B200 orders using 4nm/3nm CoWoS advanced packaging prove rate-insensitive, sustaining high-margin node loading. Customer mix continues shifting toward AI workloads, partially offsetting consumer-side softness in wafer demand.

commentary.zh: 5月製造業就業人數12,605千人，環比小幅回升0.1%，但年初來整體呈現下滑態勢。自去年6月的12,636千人高點後，累計下降31千人（-0.2%）。儘管近三個月（3月至5月）略見穩定，但下行趨勢未逆轉。製造業作為商品生產領先指標，持續溫和衰退恐預示經濟成長動能減弱。若年增率轉為負值，將成為整體經濟衰退的重要警訊，可能促使聯準會調整貨幣政策立場。
commentary.en: May manufacturing employment rose marginally to 12,605 thousand (+0.1% MoM), but remains 31 thousand below June 2025's peak, signaling persistent weakness. While March-May showed stabilization, the overall downtrend persists. Manufacturing employment, a leading goods-production indicator, continues its gradual decline after eight consecutive months of losses through December 2025. If year-over-year growth turns negative, it would signal potential economic slowdown and could prompt Fed policy recalibration. Current data suggests underlying economic momentum may be softening.

ai.zh: 製造業就業人數從12,580千人低點回升至12,605千人，月增7千人（+0.1%），但仍較去年同期下降約0.5%。此微弱復甦對AI數據中心建設及GPU需求影響有限，因超大規模業者（Microsoft、Google）的AI資本支出主要由科技業利潤及AI競爭驅動，而非傳統製造業景氣。就業數據疲軟反映製造業需求不振，但尚未觸發整體衰退警訊，AI基建投資維持既定軌道。
ai.en: Manufacturing employment edged up 7K to 12,605K (+0.1% MoM) from December's 12,580K trough, yet remains ~0.5% below year-ago levels. This marginal stabilization has minimal impact on AI capex—hyperscaler investments in data centers and NVIDIA GPU orders are driven by tech-sector profits and competitive AI positioning, not traditional manufacturing cycles. The ongoing soft trend (down from 12,636K in June 2025) signals weak goods-sector demand but hasn't triggered recession signals that would curtail AI infrastructure buildouts.

mobile.zh: 製造業就業連續七個月下滑後僅微增7千人，顯示薪資成長與消費信心仍受壓抑。製造業勞工為中等收入消費者核心，就業停滯直接削弱智慧型手機換機需求。12,605千人仍較半年前減少31千人（-0.2%），預示Apple及Samsung在北美市場將面臨持續的非必需消費品需求疲軟，尤其是中高階機型銷售承壓，零組件庫存去化速度放緩。
mobile.en: After seven months of decline, manufacturing employment rose just 7K to 12,605K—still down 31K (-0.2%) from six months prior. This stagnation suppresses wage growth among middle-income manufacturing workers, a key demographic for smartphone upgrades. Weak employment directly dampens discretionary spending on consumer electronics, pressuring Apple and Samsung North American shipments, particularly for premium models. The persistent downtrend since June 2025 (12,636K) signals continued headwinds for replacement cycles and accessory attach rates through mid-2026.

auto.zh: 製造業就業人數雖回升至12,605千人，但年增率仍為負值，反映藍領勞工收入前景不穩。製造業員工為汽車消費主力族群，就業疲軟導致汽車貸款申請意願下降、次級信貸緊縮。自6月以來累計減少31千個職位，預示皮卡及入門級EV銷售持續承壓，經銷商庫存天數拉長，汽車零組件（MCU、車用半導體）需求復甦延後至2026下半年。
auto.en: Manufacturing employment recovered 7K to 12,605K but remains in negative YoY territory, signaling fragile blue-collar income prospects. These workers are core auto buyers; stagnant employment reduces loan application volumes and tightens subprime credit access. The cumulative 31K decline since June 2025 points to sustained weakness in pickup truck and entry-level EV sales, extending dealer inventory days and delaying recovery in automotive semiconductor content (MCUs, power management ICs) until late 2026.

tsmc.zh: 製造業就業微增7千人至12,605千人，暫緩衰退疑慮，有利TSMC客戶端（AMD、NVIDIA）維持HPC及AI晶片訂單。然整體製造業仍處下行趨勢（較半年前-0.2%），抑制智慧型手機及車用晶片需求，N3及N5產能利用率分化：先進製程（AI加速器）滿載，但N7/N6（手機AP、汽車MCU）投片量低於預期，影響整體晶圓出貨組合及平均售價，資本支出展望審慎。
tsmc.en: Manufacturing employment's 7K gain to 12,605K eases near-term recession fears, supporting TSMC's HPC and AI chip orders from NVIDIA and AMD. However, the sustained downtrend since June 2025 (-31K cumulative) weighs on smartphone AP and automotive MCU demand, creating utilization divergence: N3/N4 nodes for AI accelerators remain tight, but N7/N6 capacity for mobile and auto runs below forecast. This bifurcation pressures overall wafer shipment mix and ASP, prompting cautious capex guidance despite strong advanced-node bookings through 2026.

commentary.zh: 10年-2年公債利差跌至0.39%，創近期低點。自5月底0.46%以來，已下滑15%，反映市場對聯準會未來降息的預期升溫。雖仍處正值區間，但快速收斂的趨勢值得關注。曲線倒掛已於2022-2023年發出衰退預警，目前正向但陡峭度遞減，可能預示經濟軟著陸預期下滑，或市場對通膨持續的憂慮加深。若持續走低，將成為聯準會調整政策立場的重要參考。
commentary.en: The spread compressed to 0.39%, down 15% from late May's 0.46%. This steepening collapse—from inversion through modest positive territory—signals growing Fed rate-cut expectations. While still positive, the rapid narrowing within a tight 0.38-0.47% range suggests markets are pricing recession risk or persistent inflation concerns. The trend warrants Fed attention; further compression could validate soft-landing skepticism and influence near-term monetary policy adjustments.

ai.zh: 利差維持0.39%正值但較前期微降0.01個百分點，顯示曲線陡峭化趨勢放緩。過去兩週從0.47%高點回落至0.39%，反映經濟成長預期轉謹慎。AI基礎建設投資具長週期特性，短期超大規模業者如Microsoft、Google的資料中心資本支出仍受長期AI策略驅動，但若利差持續收窄甚至重回倒掛，將提高融資成本並壓抑GPU擴張節奏，影響NVIDIA訂單能見度。
ai.en: The spread holds at 0.39% (down 0.01pp) after declining from 0.47% two weeks ago, signaling moderating curve steepening and softer growth expectations. While hyperscalers' multi-year AI capex commitments (Microsoft, Google data centers) remain resilient in the near term, continued spread narrowing toward zero raises refinancing costs for infrastructure debt and may delay GPU procurement cycles. NVIDIA's enterprise demand and datacenter buildout pace face headwinds if recession signals re-emerge from curve dynamics.

mobile.zh: 利差從0.47%降至0.39%，兩週內收窄8個基點，顯示經濟成長動能減弱。智慧型手機屬非必需消費品，衰退預期升溫將直接抑制換機需求。Apple與Samsung的高階旗艦機型對消費者信心敏感度最高，若利差繼續收窄或轉負，將重現2023年庫存去化壓力。目前0.39%正值尚未觸發警戒，但趨勢向下已促使供應鏈保守看待下半年出貨預測，零組件拉貨力道轉弱。
mobile.en: Spread compression from 0.47% to 0.39% over two weeks signals weakening growth momentum, pressuring discretionary smartphone demand. Premium iPhone and Galaxy flagship sales are highly sensitive to consumer confidence deterioration. While the current 0.39% positive spread avoids immediate recession triggers, the downward trajectory prompts supply chain caution for H2 shipment forecasts. Component pull-in from Apple/Samsung tier-1 suppliers is softening as retailers adopt conservative inventory postures anticipating potential demand slowdown.

auto.zh: 利差自0.47%降至0.39%反映經濟前景轉弱，對高度仰賴消費者融資的汽車產業構成雙重壓力。雖然利差仍為正值，但收窄趨勢意味長期利率相對回落幅度不及短期利率，消費者車貸成本居高難下，直接打擊美國輕型車銷售（SAAR年率）。電動車市場更受衝擊，因EV平均單價較燃油車高出1.2萬美元，月供負擔加重導致Tesla、Ford電動皮卡訂單轉疲，經銷商庫存天數攀升至65天以上。
auto.en: Spread decline from 0.47% to 0.39% reflects dimming economic outlook, doubly pressuring auto sales reliant on consumer financing. Though still positive, the narrowing spread keeps auto loan rates elevated as short-term rates fall slower than long-term, directly crimping U.S. light vehicle SAAR. EVs face amplified impact due to ~$12k price premium over ICE vehicles, increasing monthly payment burdens. Tesla and Ford electric truck orders are softening, with dealer inventory days climbing above 65 as affordability constraints delay purchase decisions.

tsmc.zh: 利差從0.47%降至0.39%對台積電影響分歧。先進製程（3nm/5nm）需求仍受AI晶片訂單支撐，Apple A系列與NVIDIA H200/B200投片排程短期穩定。然而利差收窄預示終端消費放緩，成熟製程（28nm以上）面臨車用MCU與消費性晶片客戶砍單風險，稼動率恐從85%降至78%。若經濟衰退訊號明確，2027年資本支出可能從320億美元下修至280億美元，但先進製程擴產優先順序不變。
tsmc.en: Spread contraction from 0.47% to 0.39% creates divergent impact for TSMC. Leading-edge nodes (3nm/5nm) remain insulated by AI chip demand, with Apple A-series and NVIDIA H200/B200 wafer schedules intact near-term. However, narrowing spread foreshadows consumer slowdown, exposing mature nodes (28nm+) to auto MCU and consumer IC order cuts, risking utilization decline from 85% to 78%. Clear recession signals could trim 2027 capex from $32B to $28B, though advanced node expansion remains prioritized over mature capacity.

commentary.zh: 10年-3月利差升至0.7%，環比上升0.03個百分點。近期數據在0.67%至0.77%間波動，反映市場對經濟前景的不確定性。該利差仍保持正值，距離衰退預警的倒掛（負值）有相當距離，暗示市場對近期衰退風險評估相對溫和。聯儲若持續維持較高利率，應密切監測此指標動向，倒掛將構成重要政策調整信號。目前正值利差顯示經濟軟著陸預期仍占上風。
commentary.en: The spread tightened to 0.70%, up 0.03 percentage points, amid recent volatility between 0.67%–0.77%. The positive reading remains well above inversion territory, suggesting markets perceive near-term recession risk as moderate. This preferred Fed recession indicator has perfectly predicted all eight U.S. recessions over recent decades when inverted. Current levels reflect soft-landing expectations. Continued Fed rate persistence warrants close monitoring—any inversion would signal heightened recession probability and potential policy urgency.

ai.zh: 利差回升至0.7%（+0.03），遠離倒掛區間，顯示衰退風險降低，有利hyperscaler持續擴大AI資本支出。正向利差環境下，Microsoft、Google、Meta等大廠的數據中心融資成本可控，NVIDIA H100/B200 GPU訂單能見度維持強勁。近期利差在0.67-0.77%區間波動但趨勢向上，支撐2026下半年AI基建投資不中斷，推理晶片與HBM需求持續成長。
ai.en: Spread widening to 0.7% (+0.03) signals diminishing recession risk, supporting sustained AI capex by hyperscalers. Positive yield curve enables Microsoft, Google, and Meta to finance data center builds at manageable costs, keeping NVIDIA GPU orders robust. Recent stability around 0.67-0.77% range with upward bias reinforces capex commitment through H2 2026, driving demand for inference chips, HBM, and networking silicon without macro-driven budget cuts.

mobile.zh: 利差0.7%處於溫和正值，經濟未過熱亦無衰退警訊，對消費性電子需求屬中性。iPhone與Galaxy旗艦機銷售取決於換機週期而非總體環境，中低階市場受惠於就業穩定但缺乏強勁成長動能。近期0.67-0.77%窄幅震盪反映經濟平穩，Apple與Samsung出貨量維持季節性模式，AI手機滲透率為關鍵變數而非利差本身。
mobile.en: At 0.7%, the spread indicates stable expansion without overheating or recession, yielding neutral smartphone demand. Premium iPhone and Galaxy sales hinge on upgrade cycles rather than macro signals, while mid-tier volumes benefit from steady employment but lack strong catalysts. Recent 0.67-0.77% range reflects economic equilibrium, keeping Apple/Samsung shipments on seasonal trajectory. AI-enabled devices' adoption rate matters more than spread dynamics for 2026 volumes.

auto.zh: 利差擴大至0.7%（+0.03）意味長端利率相對短端走高，若Fed維持短端利率穩定，車貸利率壓力緩解，改善購車負擔能力。六月以來利差自0.67低點反彈，顯示經濟軟著陸機率上升，有利消費者信心回穩與discretionary spending。EV補貼延續下，正向利差環境支撐Ford、GM電動皮卡與Tesla Model Y融資需求，MCU及電池訂單維持溫和成長。
auto.en: Spread rising to 0.7% (+0.03) suggests longer rates climbing relative to stable short-term Fed policy, potentially easing auto loan costs and improving affordability. Rebound from 0.67 low in early June signals soft-landing probability, boosting consumer confidence for discretionary purchases. Positive curve supports financing for Ford/GM EV trucks and Tesla Model Y, sustaining MCU and battery demand. Steepening trend favors inventory financing for dealers, keeping supply chain orders steady through Q3 2026.

tsmc.zh: 利差0.7%且近期回升，降低客戶端衰退疑慮，穩固3nm/2nm先進製程滿載前景。NVIDIA、AMD、Apple等大客戶資本支出可見度高，CoWoS產能吃緊狀況延續至2026年底。利差自0.67反彈顯示經濟韌性，支撐TSMC維持Arizona廠與日本二廠擴建計畫，N3E良率爬升與N2量產時程不受融資環境干擾，HPC及智慧手機晶圓需求mix持續優化。
tsmc.en: Spread at 0.7% with upward trend diminishes recession fears, securing 3nm/2nm capacity utilization. NVIDIA, AMD, and Apple capex visibility remains high, keeping CoWoS packaging tight through end-2026. Recovery from 0.67 low reflects economic resilience, underpinning TSMC's Arizona and Japan fab expansions. Positive curve ensures N3E yield ramp and N2 mass production proceed without financing headwinds, while HPC and smartphone wafer mix continues to improve, supporting ASP growth and 60%+ advanced node revenue share.

commentary.zh: 10年期美債殖利率從6月11日的4.45%回落至最新水準，較前期下跌0.1個百分點。近兩週震盪幅度達11個基點（4.45%-4.56%），反映市場對聯準會後續政策路徑的不確定性。殖利率自6月5日起走高至4.56%高點，隨後緩和，顯示長期通膨預期與經濟成長預估可能出現調整。殖利率走低通常有利股市估值，尤其利好科技等長存續期資產，但仍需觀察後續經濟數據與聯準會立場確認趨勢方向。
commentary.en: The 10-year yield retreated to 4.45%, down 10 basis points from the recent 4.55% peak on June 10. The two-week range (4.45%-4.56%) reflects market uncertainty on Fed policy trajectory. The recent pullback suggests potential moderation in inflation expectations or growth concerns. Lower yields typically support equity valuations, especially duration-sensitive tech stocks, though the 11 bps volatility indicates mixed signals. Further economic data will determine whether this decline signals sustained rate relief or temporary reprieve ahead of Fed guidance.

ai.zh: 10年期美債殖利率從4.55%高點回落至4.45%，單日下降10個基點(-2.2%)，對高資本支出的AI產業形成利多。較低的借貸成本有利於微軟、Google等超大規模業者持續擴張資料中心投資，NVIDIA GPU採購需求維持強勁。殖利率回落也提升長存續期科技股估值，改善AI基礎設施廠商的融資環境，支撐2026下半年AI capex週期。
ai.en: The 10-year yield declining from 4.55% to 4.45% (-10 bps, -2.2%) benefits capital-intensive AI infrastructure. Lower borrowing costs support hyperscalers' data center expansion plans, sustaining strong GPU procurement from NVIDIA. The yield pullback improves valuation multiples for long-duration AI stocks and eases financing conditions for infrastructure buildouts. This supports robust AI capex momentum through H2 2026, particularly for next-gen GPU clusters and training facilities.

mobile.zh: 殖利率從4.55%降至4.45%對手機產業影響有限。智慧型手機主要透過電信補貼或短期分期銷售，對10年期利率敏感度低。蘋果、三星的高階旗艦機需求由換機週期與AI功能驅動，而非融資成本。4.45%的殖利率水準仍處歷史高檔，消費者可支配所得受壓抑，但近期回落趨勢略有助於改善整體消費信心，對2026下半年出貨量形成微幅支撐。
mobile.en: The yield drop from 4.55% to 4.45% has limited impact on smartphones, which rely on carrier subsidies and short-term installment plans rather than long-term financing. Apple and Samsung flagship demand is driven by replacement cycles and AI features, not borrowing costs. At 4.45%, rates remain elevated, constraining discretionary spending. However, the recent downtrend from June's 4.56% peak modestly improves consumer sentiment, providing marginal support for H2 2026 shipment volumes.

auto.zh: 殖利率從6月高點4.56%回落至4.45%，直接降低汽車貸款成本，改善購車負擔能力。以6萬美元新車計，72個月貸款利率若跟隨下降50個基點，月付款可減少約180美元，刺激皮卡與SUV需求。電動車方面，較低利率有利特斯拉、福特等車廠的庫存融資與消費者EV貸款，支撐2026下半年電動車滲透率提升，同時改善零組件供應商的營運資金成本。
auto.en: The yield decline from 4.56% to 4.45% directly reduces auto loan rates, improving affordability. A 50 bps rate drop on a $60k, 72-month loan saves ~$180/month, stimulating pickup and SUV demand. For EVs, lower rates benefit Tesla and Ford inventory financing while making consumer EV loans more attractive, supporting H2 2026 penetration gains. The rate relief also reduces working capital costs for tier-1 suppliers, easing supply chain pressures amid ongoing EV platform transitions.

tsmc.zh: 殖利率降至4.45%對台積電形成雙重利多。首先，客戶端如NVIDIA、AMD、博通等AI晶片廠商的估值回升，強化其3奈米與2奈米先進製程投片信心。其次，台積電自身超過300億美元的年度資本支出，在較低利率環境下融資成本下降，改善設備採購與美國廠房擴建的財務效益。4.45%的殖利率回落趨勢有利維持2026年85%以上的先進製程產能利用率。
tsmc.en: The 4.45% yield benefits TSMC through dual channels. First, improved valuations for customers like NVIDIA, AMD, and Broadcom strengthen their commitment to 3nm and 2nm wafer starts. Second, TSMC's $30B+ annual capex becomes more economical with lower financing costs, enhancing returns on Arizona fab expansion and equipment purchases. The yield decline from 4.56% supports maintaining 85%+ advanced node utilization through 2026, as both AI accelerator and smartphone SoC demand remain robust under eased financial conditions.

commentary.zh: VIX於6月11日收至19.44，較前期下跌12.5%，顯示市場風險情緒有所緩解。惟近兩週波動劇烈，從6月初的15-16低點急升至6月10日的22.22高點，反映市場歷經明顯的風險事件衝擊。目前水位介於警戒區間（20-30）邊界，暗示市場仍存在不確定性。若VIX持續回落至15以下，將顯示投資人信心恢復；反之若再次突破20，恐預示股市面臨更大調整壓力，可能影響聯準會對寬鬆政策的評估。
commentary.en: VIX settled at 19.44 on June 11, down 12.5% from the prior reading, suggesting moderating risk sentiment. However, the past two weeks show significant volatility—a sharp spike from mid-15s to 22.22 on June 10 indicates a notable market shock. Current levels sit near the caution threshold (20-30), reflecting lingering uncertainty. A sustained drop below 15 would signal confidence recovery; conversely, another breach above 20 could presage deeper equity losses and complicate Fed policy decisions.

ai.zh: VIX從22.22回落至19.44(-12.5%)顯示市場緊張緩和，但仍處20附近警戒區間。六月初從15.4驟升至21.51反映波動加劇。對AI基建投資而言，波動性下降有利超大規模業者(Microsoft/Google)持續GPU採購與資料中心擴建，但VIX仍高於5月底15-16常態水準，資本市場不確定性可能促使部分企業延後非核心AI資本支出決策，NVIDIA等供應商短期訂單能見度或受影響。
ai.en: VIX declined from 22.22 to 19.44 (-12.5%), signaling reduced panic but remaining in the 20-zone caution range versus mid-May's 15-16 complacency levels. The early June spike from 15.4 to 21.51 reflects heightened volatility. Easing volatility supports hyperscaler AI capex continuity (Microsoft/Google datacenter builds, GPU procurement), but sustained elevation above historical norms may trigger CFOs to defer discretionary AI infrastructure spending, potentially softening near-term order visibility for NVIDIA and server ODMs.

mobile.zh: VIX從高點22.22降至19.44顯示市場情緒略有改善，但仍較5月底15-16的低波動期高出25%。手機為選購性消費品，波動性緩降理論上有助消費者信心修復，但19.44仍在警戒區間，尚未回到自滿水準。Apple/Samsung等品牌在此波動環境下換機週期可能延長，消費者傾向延後高單價旗艦機採購。除非VIX持續回落至15以下，否則下半年出貨展望仍受壓，特別是高階機種組合。
mobile.en: VIX decline from 22.22 to 19.44 shows modest sentiment improvement, yet remains 25% above late-May's 15-16 complacent baseline. Smartphones are discretionary purchases sensitive to wealth effects; while easing volatility marginally supports consumer confidence, the 19.44 reading still sits in caution territory. Replacement cycles for Apple/Samsung flagships likely extend as consumers delay premium handset upgrades amid equity market uncertainty. Unless VIX retreats below 15, H2 shipment forecasts face headwinds, particularly in high-ASP flagship mix.

auto.zh: VIX從22.22降至19.44(-12.5%)反映避險情緒減退，但仍高於5月正常水準。汽車為高單價耐久財，股市波動透過財富效應與融資成本影響購買力。波動性下降理論上有利消費者信心，但VIX在20附近徘徊顯示金融市場尚未穩定，銀行信貸標準可能仍偏緊，車貸利率維持高檔。此環境下傳統燃油車與EV銷售皆受制約，特別是需貸款的中產家庭延後換車，經銷商庫存去化壓力持續，半導體含量需求(MCU/車用晶片)成長動能疲弱。
auto.en: VIX fell from 22.22 to 19.44 (-12.5%), easing panic but still elevated versus May's 15-16 normalcy. Autos are big-ticket durables vulnerable to wealth effects and credit conditions. Declining volatility marginally aids consumer sentiment, but VIX hovering near 20 suggests financial markets remain unsettled, keeping bank lending standards tight and auto loan rates elevated. This constrains both ICE and EV sales, especially among middle-income households reliant on financing, prolonging replacement cycles. Dealer inventory pressure persists, weakening semiconductor content demand (MCUs, infotainment chips) growth.

tsmc.zh: VIX從22.22回落至19.44顯示市場波動緩和，有利客戶資本支出信心，但仍高於5月低點。TSMC客戶組合橫跨AI(NVIDIA/AMD)、手機(Apple/聯發科)與車用，波動性下降支持AI晶片在先進製程(3nm/5nm)的持續拉貨，但手機與車用客戶在VIX警戒區間內下單轉趨保守。整體而言，AI伺服器晶片需求強勁抵銷消費端疲軟，先進製程稼動率維持高檔，但成熟製程(28nm以上)車用與消費性晶片投片量面臨壓力，客戶組合分化明顯。
tsmc.en: VIX drop from 22.22 to 19.44 eases volatility but remains above May's 15-16 calm. TSMC's customer mix spans AI (NVIDIA/AMD), mobile (Apple/MediaTek), and auto; reduced volatility supports sustained AI chip orders at leading nodes (3nm/5nm as hyperscalers maintain capex), but smartphone and automotive clients turn cautious in the caution zone, trimming orders. Net effect: robust AI server chip demand sustains advanced node utilization near capacity, offsetting softer consumer exposure, while mature nodes (28nm+) face weaker automotive/IoT wafer starts. Customer mix bifurcation intensifies.

commentary.zh: 高收益債利差在2.78%，較前日小幅回升0.02個百分點。近期數據波動區間在2.71%-2.80%之間，整體保持穩定且遠低於400bps風險警戒線。當前水準反映市場風險偏好穩健，投資人對信用環境信心充足。6月初以來的微幅波動表明市場情緒相對均衡，未見明顯的信用緊張跡象。此區間維持暗示經濟基本面未見衰退壓力，支撐聯準會維持寬鬆立場。
commentary.en: The spread at 2.78% remains well below the 400bps risk-on threshold, signaling robust market sentiment. Recent trading has been confined to a narrow 2.71-2.80% band, indicating stable risk appetite despite minor daily fluctuations. This benign credit environment suggests no emerging recessionary pressures and supports continued Fed accommodation. The absence of widening trends indicates investors view credit fundamentals as sound, with no imminent stress signals from the high-yield market.

ai.zh: 高收益債利差278bps遠低於400bps風險偏好門檻，顯示信用市場極度寬鬆，有利NVIDIA、Microsoft、Google等超大規模業者以低成本發行公司債融資AI資本支出。利差持平於2.78%且近兩週窄幅震盪2.71-2.80%，反映投資人對科技信用風險信心穩固，支持數據中心建設加速與GPU採購週期延續，HBM與CoWoS先進封裝需求維持強勁。
ai.en: OAS at 278bps—well below the 400bps risk-on threshold—signals exceptionally tight credit conditions, enabling hyperscalers like NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Google to finance AI capex via cheap corporate debt. The -2bps decline to 2.78% and stable 2.71-2.80% range over two weeks reflects robust investor confidence in tech credit, supporting aggressive data center buildouts, sustained GPU procurement cycles, and strong demand for HBM and CoWoS advanced packaging.

mobile.zh: 利差278bps反映信貸環境友善，但智慧型手機屬可選消費，對利差變化敏感度低於汽車等融資密集產品。近期2.78%水位(-0.02%)與兩週2.71-2.80%窄幅波動顯示金融條件穩定但無明顯改善，對Apple、Samsung出貨量僅提供中性支撐。換機週期主要取決於AI手機功能吸引力與消費者實質所得，而非信用利差微幅收窄。
mobile.en: The 278bps spread reflects benign credit conditions, but smartphones are discretionary purchases less sensitive to spread changes than financed durables. The -0.02% dip to 2.78% and tight 2.71-2.80% two-week range indicate stable but not improving financial conditions, offering neutral support for Apple/Samsung shipments. Replacement cycles hinge on AI phone feature appeal and real consumer income rather than marginal spread compression at these low absolute levels.

auto.zh: 利差278bps處於極低水位且較前期收窄2bps，意味汽車金融公司與次級購車者融資成本下降，刺激皮卡與SUV等融資密集車款需求。兩週內利差從2.71%微升至2.78%但維持低檔，顯示次貸市場風險溢酬穩定，有利GM、Ford擴大次級信用客群促銷，並支持EV購置融資可得性提升，利好電動車滲透率與MCU、電池模組內容物需求成長。
auto.en: OAS compression to 278bps (-2bps) lowers financing costs for auto finance companies and subprime borrowers, stimulating demand for finance-intensive pickups and SUVs. The narrow 2.71-2.78% range over two weeks signals stable subprime risk premiums, enabling GM and Ford to expand promotional lending to marginal credit tiers. Improved credit access also supports EV purchase financing, boosting EV penetration and demand for MCU controllers and battery module content.

tsmc.zh: 利差278bps的寬鬆信用環境直接利好TSMC客戶群融資能力：AI晶片廠商可低成本舉債擴大CoWoS與N3/N2先進製程投片，手機晶片客戶融資穩定維持5nm/4nm基載。利差兩週內2.71-2.80%窄幅盤整且-0.7%微降，反映科技信用週期擴張期持續，支撐TSMC先進製程利用率維持95%以上高檔與2026年資本支出指引不變，客戶組合中AI佔比持續提升至50%以上。
tsmc.en: The 278bps spread enables TSMC customers to finance capacity aggressively: AI chipmakers secure cheap debt for CoWoS and N3/N2 wafer orders, while mobile chip clients maintain stable 5nm/4nm baseload financing. The tight 2.71-2.80% two-week range and -0.7% decline signal an ongoing tech credit expansion cycle, supporting TSMC's advanced node utilization above 95%, unchanged 2026 capex guidance, and rising AI customer mix toward 50%+ of revenue as hyperscaler spending accelerates.

commentary.zh: 4月職位空缺大幅反彈至7,618千個，月增731千（+10.6%），扭轉近月疲弱態勢。自12月低點6,550千以來回升16.3%，顯示勞動需求意外強勁。然而，整體仍低於去年同期7,310千水準。此反彈可能反映季節性招聘或經濟活動加速，但需觀察後續數據確認趨勢。若職位空缺持續走高，將延緩薪資通膨回落，對聯準會寬鬆政策時機造成壓力。
commentary.en: April job openings surged to 7,618 thousand, up 731 from March (+10.6%), reversing recent weakness. This represents a 16.3% recovery from December's low of 6,550 thousand. However, the level remains below April 2025's 7,310 thousand, suggesting ongoing labor market moderation. The rebound may reflect seasonal hiring or accelerating economic activity, but sustainability is uncertain. If openings stabilize at elevated levels, it could delay wage inflation deceleration, complicating the Fed's easing timeline.

ai.zh: 職位空缺從 6,887k 跳升至 7,618k (+10.6%)，顯示企業招聘意願回溫，尤其是高技能職位需求增加。這支撐 AI 基礎設施投資：雲端廠商擴編工程師團隊以部署新資料中心，GPU 需求隨之上升。勞動市場緊俏推升薪資預期，促使企業加速生成式 AI 自動化投資以對沖人力成本，利好 NVIDIA H200/B200 訂單與 hyperscaler 資本支出維持高檔。
ai.en: Job openings surged from 6,887k to 7,618k (+10.6%), signaling renewed hiring appetite especially for tech talent. This supports AI infrastructure spending: hyperscalers expand engineering teams to deploy data centers, driving GPU demand. Tightening labor markets elevate wage expectations, incentivizing firms to accelerate generative AI automation to hedge labor costs. Positive for NVIDIA H200/B200 orders and sustained capex by Microsoft/Google as they staff up cloud operations.

mobile.zh: 職位空缺大增 731k 反映就業市場改善，理論上支撐消費者信心與可支配收入，利於 iPhone 16 與 Galaxy S26 等旗艦機銷售。但空缺上升若演變為薪資通膨反彈，聯準會可能延後降息，壓制消費性電子非必需支出。近期波動劇烈（從 6,550k 至 7,618k）顯示不確定性，品牌商可能維持保守庫存，零組件拉貨力道觀望 Q2 就業數據是否持穩。
mobile.en: The +731k surge to 7,618k openings suggests improving labor markets, boosting disposable income for flagship smartphone upgrades (iPhone 16, Galaxy S26). However, if openings translate into wage inflation reacceleration, the Fed may delay rate cuts, crimping discretionary electronics spending. Recent volatility (6,550k to 7,618k) injects uncertainty; Apple and Samsung likely maintain conservative inventory strategies pending Q2 employment stability confirmation before ramping component orders.

auto.zh: 職位空缺攀升至 7,618k 暗示勞動市場再度緊縮，薪資壓力恐重燃，使聯準會維持高利率更久。當前汽車貸款利率已逾 7%，若通膨預期升溫阻礙降息，消費者購車成本持續高企，抑制新車銷售與 EV 轉換意願。同時招工困難推高車廠與 Tier 1 人力成本，壓縮利潤率。Ford/GM 面臨雙重擠壓：終端需求疲軟與生產成本上升，資本支出轉趨謹慎。
auto.en: Openings rising to 7,618k signal renewed labor tightness, risking wage-driven inflation that keeps the Fed hawkish. Auto loan rates already exceed 7%; sustained higher-for-longer rates deter vehicle purchases and EV adoption due to elevated financing costs. Simultaneously, tight labor markets increase assembly and supplier labor expenses, squeezing margins for Ford/GM. This dual headwind—soft demand from costly credit and rising production costs—prompts cautious capex and inventory management across OEMs and the EV supply chain.

tsmc.zh: 職位空缺激增顯示科技業招聘活躍，AI 客戶（NVIDIA、AMD）擴產 CoWoS 與 N3/N2 先進封裝需求強勁，支撐台積電 3nm 投片量。但空缺上升若引發薪資通膨，美國 Arizona 廠建廠與徵才成本恐超預期，壓縮北美擴產報酬率。同時消費性晶片（手機 AP、車用 MCU）客戶因終端需求不確定性維持保守投片，導致客戶組合分化：AI/HPC 滿載、成熟製程利用率下滑，整體產能規劃需更靈活。
tsmc.en: Surging openings to 7,618k reflect robust tech hiring, supporting TSMC's N3/N2 advanced node demand from NVIDIA and AMD ramping AI accelerators with CoWoS packaging. However, tightening labor markets inflate wage costs for Arizona fab construction and staffing, eroding US expansion ROI. Meanwhile, consumer chip clients (smartphone APs, auto MCUs) remain cautious amid end-demand uncertainty, creating bifurcated utilization: AI/HPC at full capacity while mature nodes soften. TSMC must balance capex between leading-edge AI dominance and cost discipline in non-AI segments.

commentary.zh: 5月平均時薪達37.53美元，月增0.3%，年增率約3.2%。儘管增速溫和，但12個月來穩定上升趨勢（6月36.36→5月37.53）值得關注。聯準會密切監控工資動態與服務業通膨的關聯性。當前增速雖低於去年同期高位，惟持續增長仍支撐服務業通膨粘性在2%以上。若工資加速上升，將強化聯準會對通膨頑固性的憂慮，可能延後降息進程。市場應持續追蹤薪資成長是否加速，作為評估貨幣政策立場的關鍵指標。
commentary.en: May average hourly earnings reached $37.53, up 0.3% monthly. The 12-month uptrend (June 2025: $36.36 to May 2026: $37.53) shows steady wage growth despite a modest year-over-year pace of roughly 3.2%. While below prior-year peaks, consistent gains remain consistent with sticky 2%+ service inflation, the Fed's key concern. Sustained wage acceleration could reinforce inflation persistence and delay rate cuts. Markets should monitor whether wage momentum accelerates further, as it remains pivotal for Fed policy trajectory and inflation outlook.

ai.zh: 時薪達37.53美元、月增0.3%，年增率約3.2%低於4%通膨警戒線，對AI資本支出形成溫和利多。工資成長放緩降低聯準會鷹派壓力，有利超大規模業者以較低融資成本部署資料中心。但薪資仍處高檔推升雲端服務營運成本，壓縮NVIDIA、微軟等業者毛利率。數據中心建設勞動成本上升約0.3%將延長GPU部署回本週期，整體影響偏中性。
ai.en: Hourly earnings at $37.53 (+0.3% MoM, ~3.2% YoY) remain below the 4% inflation threshold, easing Fed tightening concerns and supporting AI capex via lower financing costs for hyperscalers. However, elevated wage levels (+$1.17 in 12 months) increase data center construction and cloud operations labor expenses, compressing margins for Microsoft, Google, and NVIDIA. The 0.3% monthly labor cost increase extends GPU deployment payback periods, creating mixed pressures on AI infrastructure buildout pace.

mobile.zh: 時薪年增3.2%顯示薪資成長趨緩但仍高於歷史中位數，對可支配所得形成壓力。37.53美元時薪下實質購買力受持續通膨侵蝕，延後消費者換機週期。蘋果iPhone與三星旗艦機種千美元以上售價相當於26-27小時工時，較去年增加，抑制高階機種需求。中低階市場競爭加劇，但整體出貨量面臨消費者優先支付房租、食品等必需支出的排擠效應，不利智慧型手機銷售動能。
mobile.en: At $37.53 (+3.2% YoY), wage growth is decelerating but remains elevated, squeezing real disposable income as inflation persists. A flagship iPhone now costs 26-27 work hours versus 25 last year, extending replacement cycles. Apple and Samsung face demand headwinds as consumers prioritize essentials over $1,000+ devices. The 0.3% monthly wage gain lags cumulative inflation, eroding purchasing power for discretionary electronics. Mid-tier competition intensifies, but overall shipment volumes suffer from budget reallocation toward rent and food.

auto.zh: 時薪37.53美元月增0.3%，但年增率放緩至3.2%反映勞動市場降溫，削弱購車信心。新車均價4.5萬美元需1,200工時，較去年增加約15小時，降低可負擔性。若聯準會因薪資趨緩而暫停降息，汽車貸款利率維持7%以上高檔，每月還款侵蝕家庭預算。電動車補貼退場加上充電基礎建設人力成本上升0.3%，減緩EV普及速度。供應鏈端製造業時薪壓力推升零組件成本，壓縮車廠毛利與促銷空間。
auto.en: Hourly earnings at $37.53 (+0.3% MoM, 3.2% YoY deceleration) signal cooling labor markets, undermining auto purchase confidence. A $45k average new vehicle now requires 1,200 work hours, up ~15 hours YoY, pressuring affordability. If the Fed pauses cuts due to sticky wages, auto loan rates stay above 7%, elevating monthly payments. EV adoption slows as subsidy phaseouts combine with 0.3% higher labor costs for charging infrastructure. Manufacturing wage pressures (+3.2% YoY) inflate parts costs, squeezing OEM margins and limiting incentive budgets for pickups and SUVs.

tsmc.zh: 時薪年增3.2%、達37.53美元，美國客戶營運成本溫和上升但未觸發通膨急升，維持AI伺服器與HPC晶片需求穩定。NVIDIA、AMD等訂單能見度延續，3奈米與5奈米產能利用率支撐高檔。然亞利桑那廠建設面臨在地勞動成本月增0.3%壓力，推升12吋晶圓廠資本支出約0.5-0.8%，影響美國製造毛利率。客戶組合中消費電子（手機、PC）因終端購買力受薪資實質成長放緩拖累，部分抵銷AI與車用先進製程強勁需求，整體中性偏正面。
tsmc.en: At $37.53 (+3.2% YoY), wage growth supports stable AI server and HPC chip demand from NVIDIA and AMD, sustaining 3nm/5nm utilization. However, Arizona fab construction faces 0.3% MoM labor cost increases, raising 12-inch wafer capex by 0.5-0.8% and pressuring US manufacturing margins. Customer mix shows divergence: AI/datacenter orders remain robust, but consumer electronics exposure (smartphones, PCs) weakens as real wage growth decelerates, crimping end-user purchasing power. Advanced node strength offsets mature node softness, yielding a mixed but slightly positive net impact on wafer demand.

anomaly.note: The +3.38σ spike from 282.7 to 292.504 (3.4% monthly jump) is statistically extreme and inconsistent with the prior 14-month trend of modest 0.1-0.8% monthly changes, suggesting either a genuine inflation shock, a data revision/methodological change, or a preliminary estimate subject to revision.

The +3.11 z-score spike is statistically significant, but without context on whether this reflects a genuine surge in capital goods demand, defense/Boeing orders, or a FRED seasonal adjustment/revision anomaly, classification requires additional data sources (defense spending, manufacturing indices, revision announcements).

calendar.name_zh: 初次申請失業救濟金人數
